141	class SglFunction:
142	    def __init__(self, func, num_api_spec_tokens=None, bind_arguments=None):
143	        self.func = func
144	        self.num_api_spec_tokens = num_api_spec_tokens
145	        self.bind_arguments = bind_arguments or {}
146	        self.pin_prefix_rid = None
147	
148	        # Parse arguments
149	        argspec = inspect.getfullargspec(func)
150	        assert argspec.args[0] == "s", 'The first argument must be "s"'
151	        self.arg_names = argspec.args[1:]
152	        self.arg_defaults = argspec.defaults if argspec.defaults is not None else []
153	
154	    def bind(self, **kwargs):
155	        assert all(key in self.arg_names for key in kwargs)
156	
157	        new_bind_dict = {**self.bind_arguments, **kwargs}
158	        return SglFunction(self.func, bind_arguments=new_bind_dict)
159	
160	    def run(
161	        self,
162	        *args,
163	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
164	        n: int = 1,
165	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
166	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
167	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
168	        temperature: float = 1.0,
169	        top_p: float = 1.0,
170	        top_k: int = -1,
171	        min_p: float = 0.0,
172	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
173	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
174	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
175	        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
176	        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
177	        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
178	        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
179	        stream: bool = False,
180	        backend=None,
181	        use_thread: bool = True,
182	        **kwargs,
183	    ):
184	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import run_program
185	
186	        # avoid using [] as the default arg: https://nikos7am.com/posts/mutable-default-arguments/
187	        if stop is None:
188	            stop = []
189	        if stop_token_ids is None:
190	            stop_token_ids = []
191	        if stop_regex is None:
192	            stop_regex = []
193	
194	        default_sampling_para = SglSamplingParams(
195	            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
196	            n=n,
197	            stop=stop,
198	            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
199	            stop_regex=stop_regex,
200	            temperature=temperature,
201	            top_p=top_p,
202	            top_k=top_k,
203	            min_p=min_p,
204	            frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
205	            presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
206	            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
207	            return_logprob=return_logprob,
208	            logprob_start_len=logprob_start_len,
209	            top_logprobs_num=top_logprobs_num,
210	            return_text_in_logprobs=return_text_in_logprobs,
211	        )
212	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
213	        return run_program(
214	            self,
215	            backend,
216	            args,
217	            kwargs,
218	            default_sampling_para,
219	            stream,
220	            use_thread=use_thread,
221	        )
222	
223	    def run_batch(
224	        self,
225	        batch_kwargs,
226	        *,
227	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
228	        n: int = 1,
229	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
230	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
231	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
232	        temperature: float = 1.0,
233	        top_p: float = 1.0,
234	        top_k: int = -1,
235	        min_p: float = 0.0,
236	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
237	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
238	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
239	        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
240	        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
241	        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
242	        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
243	        backend=None,
244	        num_threads: Union[str, int] = "auto",
245	        progress_bar: bool = False,
246	        generator_style: bool = False,
247	    ):
248	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import run_program_batch
249	
250	        if stop is None:
251	            stop = []
252	        if stop_token_ids is None:
253	            stop_token_ids = []
254	        if stop_regex is None:
255	            stop_regex = []
256	
257	        assert isinstance(batch_kwargs, (list, tuple))
258	        if len(batch_kwargs) == 0:
259	            return []
260	        if not isinstance(batch_kwargs[0], dict):
261	            num_programs = len(batch_kwargs)
262	            # change the list of argument values to dict of arg_name -> arg_value
263	            batch_kwargs = [
264	                {self.arg_names[i]: v for i, v in enumerate(arg_values)}
265	                for arg_values in batch_kwargs
266	                if isinstance(arg_values, (list, tuple))
267	                and len(self.arg_names) - len(self.arg_defaults)

332	        self.prev_node = None
333	        self.pid = None
334	        SglExpr.node_ct += 1

336	    def __add__(self, other):
337	        if isinstance(other, str):
338	            other = SglConstantText(other)
339	        assert isinstance(other, SglExpr)
340	
341	        return self.concatenate_ir(self, other)

343	    def __radd__(self, other):
344	        if isinstance(other, str):

347

350	    def concatenate_ir(self, a, b):
351	        if isinstance(a, SglExprList):
352	            if isinstance(b, SglExprList):
353	                return SglExprList(a.expr_list + b.expr_list)
354	            else:
355	                return SglExprList(a.expr_list + [b])
356	        elif isinstance(b, SglExprList):
357	            return SglExprList([a] + b.expr_list)
358	
359	        return SglExprList([a, b])
360	
361	    def print_graph_dfs(self):
362	        ret = [""]
363	        visited = set()
364	
365	        def dfs_print(x):
366	            if x is None or x in visited:
367	                return
368	            visited.add(x)
369	
370	            # Print dependency
371	            if x.prev_node is not None:
372	                dfs_print(x.prev_node)
373	
374	            if isinstance(x, SglExprList):
375	                for y in x.expr_list:
376	                    dfs_print(y)
377	            # elif isinstance(x, SglRole):
378	            #    dfs_print(x.expr)
379	            elif isinstance(x, SglVariable):
380	                dfs_print(x.source)
381	
382	            # Print the node itself
383	            if isinstance(x, (SglFork, SglGetForkItem)):
384	                ret[0] += f"%{x.node_id} = {x}\n"
385	            else:
386	                if x.prev_node is not None:
387	                    ret[0] += (
388	                        f"%{x.node_id} = %{x.prev_node.node_id} + " + str(x) + "\n"
389	                    )
390	                else:
391	                    ret[0] += f"%{x.node_id} = " + str(x) + "\n"
392	
393	        dfs_print(self)
394	        return ret[0]

42	def run_internal(state, program, func_args, func_kwargs, sync):
43	    try:
44	        state.ret_value = program.func(state, *func_args, **func_kwargs)
45	    except Exception as e:
46	        raise e
47	    finally:
48	        state.stream_executor.end()
49	
50	    if sync:
51	        state.stream_executor.sync()
52	
53	    if global_config.verbosity >= 2:
54	        print(state.text())
55

57	def run_program(
58	    program,
59	    backend,
60	    func_args,
61	    func_kwargs,
62	    default_sampling_para,
63	    stream,
64	    sync=False,
65	    use_thread=True,
66	):
67	    if hasattr(backend, "endpoint"):
68	        backend = backend.endpoint
69	    assert backend is not None, "Please specify a backend"
70	    func_kwargs.update(program.bind_arguments)
71	    stream_executor = StreamExecutor(
72	        backend,
73	        func_kwargs,
74	        default_sampling_para,
75	        chat_template=None,
76	        stream=stream,
77	        num_api_spec_tokens=program.num_api_spec_tokens,
78	        use_thread=use_thread,
79	    )
80	    state = ProgramState(stream_executor)
81	
82	    if stream:
83	        t = threading.Thread(
84	            target=run_internal, args=(state, program, func_args, func_kwargs, sync)
85	        )
86	        t.start()
87	        return state
88	    else:
89	        run_internal(state, program, func_args, func_kwargs, sync)
90	        return state
91

93	def run_program_batch(

96	    batch_arguments,
97	    default_sampling_para,
98	    num_threads,
99	    progress_bar,
100	    generator_style=False,
101	):
102	    if hasattr(backend, "endpoint"):
103	        backend = backend.endpoint
104	
105	    # Pre-cache the common prefix for a batch. The prefix is extracted by tracing the program.
106	    if global_config.enable_precache_with_tracing and len(batch_arguments) > 1:
107	        cache_program(program, backend)
108	
109	    # Run all programs
110	    if num_threads == "auto":
111	        num_threads = max(96, multiprocessing.cpu_count() * 16)
112	    num_threads = min(num_threads, len(batch_arguments))

56	class GreedyTokenSelection(ChoicesSamplingMethod):
57	
58	    def __call__(
59	        self,
60	        *,
61	        choices: List[str],
62	        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
63	        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
64	        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
65	        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
66	    ) -> ChoicesDecision:
67	        """Select the option based on greedy logprob selection. For overlapping options
68	        where one option is a subset of a longer option, extend the shorter option using
69	        its average logprob for comparison against the longer option."""
70	
71	        num_options = len(choices)
72	        max_tokens = max(len(option) for option in input_token_logprobs)
73	        logprob_matrix = self._build_logprob_matrix(
74	            input_token_logprobs, max_tokens, num_options
75	        )
76	        remaining = self._greedy_selection(logprob_matrix, num_options, max_tokens)
77	
78	        best_choice = choices[remaining[0]]
79	        meta_info = {
80	            "normalized_prompt_logprobs": normalized_prompt_logprobs,
81	            "input_token_logprobs": input_token_logprobs,
82	            "output_token_logprobs": output_token_logprobs,
83	            "greedy_logprob_matrix": logprob_matrix.tolist(),
84	        }
85	        return ChoicesDecision(decision=best_choice, meta_info=meta_info)
86	
87	    def _build_logprob_matrix(self, input_token_logprobs, max_tokens, num_options):
88	        logprob_matrix = np.zeros((num_options, max_tokens))
89	        for i, option in enumerate(input_token_logprobs):
90	            actual_logprobs = [token[0] for token in option]
91	            avg_logprob = np.mean(actual_logprobs)
92	            logprob_matrix[i, : len(option)] = actual_logprobs
93	            if len(option) < max_tokens:
94	                logprob_matrix[i, len(option) :] = avg_logprob
95	        return logprob_matrix
96	
97	    def _greedy_selection(self, logprob_matrix, num_options, max_tokens):
98	        remaining = np.arange(num_options)
99	        for j in range(max_tokens):
100	            max_logprob = np.max(logprob_matrix[remaining, j])
101	            remaining = remaining[logprob_matrix[remaining, j] == max_logprob]
102	            if len(remaining) == 1:
103	                break
104	        return remaining
105	
106	
107	greedy_token_selection = GreedyTokenSelection()

110	class UnconditionalLikelihoodNormalized(ChoicesSamplingMethod):
111	
112	    @property
113	    def requires_unconditional_logprobs(self) -> bool:
114	        return True
115	
116	    def __call__(
117	        self,
118	        *,
119	        choices: List[str],
120	        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
121	        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
122	        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
123	        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
124	    ) -> ChoicesDecision:
125	        """Select the option with the highest average token logprob once normalized by
126	        the unconditional token logprobs.
127	
128	        The first unconditional token logprob is assumed to be None. If so, it is
129	        replaced with 0 for the purposes of normalization."""
130	
131	        if unconditional_token_logprobs is None:
132	            raise ValueError(
133	                "Unconditional token logprobs are required for this method."

150	    def _normalize_logprobs(self, input_token_logprobs, unconditional_token_logprobs):
151	        normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs = []
152	        for inputs, unconditionals in zip(
153	            input_token_logprobs, unconditional_token_logprobs
154	        ):
155	            inputs_logprobs = np.array([token[0] for token in inputs])
156	            unconditionals_logprobs = np.array([token[0] for token in unconditionals])
157	            unconditionals_logprobs[0] = unconditionals_logprobs[0] or 0
158	            normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs.append(
159	                float(np.mean(inputs_logprobs - unconditionals_logprobs))
160	            )
161	        return normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs

184	def _run_program_batch_generator(
185	    program,
186	    backend,
187	    batch_arguments,
188	    default_sampling_para,
189	    num_threads,
190	    progress_bar,
191	):
192	    """Helper function that yields results one by one using chunking to avoid overwhelming ThreadPoolExecutor."""
193	    if num_threads == 1:
194	        iterator = tqdm.tqdm(batch_arguments) if progress_bar else batch_arguments
195	        for arguments in iterator:
196	            yield run_program(
197	                program,
198	                backend,
199	                (),
200	                arguments,
201	                default_sampling_para,
202	                False,
203	                True,
204	            )
205	    else:
206	        pbar = tqdm.tqdm(total=len(batch_arguments)) if progress_bar else None
207	
208	        # Process in chunks to avoid overwhelming ThreadPoolExecutor
209	        # Otherwise, ThreadPoolExecutor.submit will block after adding certain number of tasks
210	        # so we will never reach "yield" until all tasks are done
211	        chunk_size = 200
212	
213	        with ThreadPoolExecutor(num_threads) as executor:
214	            for chunk_start in range(0, len(batch_arguments), chunk_size):
215	                chunk_end = min(chunk_start + chunk_size, len(batch_arguments))
216	                chunk_futures = []
217	
218	                # Submit chunk of tasks
219	                for i in range(chunk_start, chunk_end):
220	                    future = executor.submit(
221	                        run_program,
222	                        program,
223	                        backend,
224	                        (),
225	                        batch_arguments[i],
226	                        default_sampling_para,
227	                        False,
228	                        True,
229	                    )
230	                    if pbar:
231	                        future.add_done_callback(lambda _: pbar.update())
232	                    chunk_futures.append(future)
233	
234	                # Yield results from this chunk as they complete
235	                for future in chunk_futures:
236	                    yield future.result()
237	
238	        if pbar:
239	            pbar.close()

519	    def _spec_gen(self, sampling_params):
520	        stop = sampling_params.stop
521	        max_new_tokens = sampling_params.max_new_tokens
522	        meta_info = {}
523	
524	        def regen():
525	            nonlocal meta_info
526	
527	            sampling_params.max_new_tokens = max(
528	                sampling_params.max_new_tokens, self.num_api_spec_tokens
529	            )
530	            sampling_params.stop = None
531	            self.speculated_text, meta_info = self.backend.generate(
532	                self, sampling_params=sampling_params
533	            )
534	
535	        def find_stop():
536	            if isinstance(stop, str):
537	                return self.speculated_text.find(stop)
538	            elif isinstance(stop, (tuple, list)):
539	                pos = -1
540	                for stop_str in stop:
541	                    stop_pos = self.speculated_text.find(stop_str)
542	                    if stop_pos != -1 and (pos == -1 or stop_pos < pos):
543	                        pos = stop_pos
544	                return pos
545	            else:
546	                raise Exception("Wrong type of stop in sampling parameters.")
547	
548	        if stop is None:
549	            if len(self.speculated_text) < max_new_tokens:
550	                regen()
551	            comp = self.speculated_text[:max_new_tokens]
552	            self.speculated_text = self.speculated_text[max_new_tokens:]
553	        elif isinstance(stop, (str, list, tuple)):
554	            if self.speculated_text == "":
555	                regen()
556	            stop_pos = find_stop()
557	            if stop_pos == -1:
558	                stop_pos = min(
559	                    sampling_params.max_new_tokens,
560	                    len(self.speculated_text),
561	                )
562	            comp = self.speculated_text[:stop_pos]
563	            self.speculated_text = self.speculated_text[stop_pos:]
564	        else:
565	            raise ValueError("Wrong type of stop in sampling parameters.")

16	import logging
17	from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Union
18

25	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-6
26	TOP_K_ALL = 1 << 30
27	
28	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
29	
30	
31	class SamplingParams:
32	    """
33	    The sampling parameters.
34	
35	    See docs/backend/sampling_params.md or
36	    https://docs.sglang.io/backend/sampling_params.html
37	    for the documentation.
38	    """
39	
40	    def __init__(
41	        self,
42	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
43	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
44	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
45	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
46	        temperature: float = 1.0,
47	        top_p: float = 1.0,
48	        top_k: int = -1,
49	        min_p: float = 0.0,
50	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
51	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
52	        repetition_penalty: float = 1.0,
53	        min_new_tokens: int = 0,
54	        n: int = 1,
55	        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
56	        regex: Optional[str] = None,
57	        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
58	        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
59	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
60	        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
61	        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
62	        no_stop_trim: bool = False,
63	        custom_params: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
64	        stream_interval: Optional[int] = None,
65	        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None,
66	        sampling_seed: Optional[int] = None,
67	    ) -> None:
68	        self.max_new_tokens = max_new_tokens
69	        self.stop_strs = stop
70	        if stop_token_ids:
71	            self.stop_token_ids = set(stop_token_ids)
72	        else:
73	            self.stop_token_ids = None
74	        self.stop_regex_strs = stop_regex
75	        self.temperature = temperature
76	        self.top_p = top_p
77	        self.top_k = top_k
78	        self.min_p = min_p
79	        self.frequency_penalty = frequency_penalty
80	        self.presence_penalty = presence_penalty
81	        self.repetition_penalty = repetition_penalty
82	        self.min_new_tokens = min_new_tokens
83	        self.regex = regex
84	        self.n = n
85	        self.json_schema = json_schema
86	        self.ebnf = ebnf
87	        self.structural_tag = structural_tag
88	        self.ignore_eos = ignore_eos
89	        self.skip_special_tokens = skip_special_tokens
90	        self.spaces_between_special_tokens = spaces_between_special_tokens
91	        self.no_stop_trim = no_stop_trim
92	        self.custom_params = custom_params
93	        self.stream_interval = stream_interval
94	        self.logit_bias = logit_bias
95	        self.sampling_seed = sampling_seed
96	
97	        # Process some special cases
98	        if 0 <= self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
99	            # top_k = 1 means greedy sampling
100	            self.temperature = 1.0
101	            self.top_k = 1
102	        if self.top_k == -1:
103	            self.top_k = TOP_K_ALL  # whole vocabulary
104

106	        if self.temperature < 0.0:

109	            )
110	        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:

112	        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:

117	            )

123	        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:

126	            )
127	        if not 0.0 <= self.repetition_penalty <= 2.0:

20	def schema_gen(s, message: Tuple[str, str], json_schema: str):
21	    system, user = message
22	    s += sgl.system(system)
23	    s += sgl.user(user)
24	    s += sgl.assistant(
25	        sgl.gen("json_output", temperature=0, max_tokens=256, json_schema=json_schema)
26	    )

29	def contains_formats(schema, formats: List[str]):
30	    if isinstance(schema, dict):
31	        if schema.get("format", None) in formats:
32	            return True
33	        for value in schema.values():
34	            if contains_formats(value, formats):
35	                return True
36	    elif isinstance(schema, list):
37	        for item in schema:
38	            if contains_formats(item, formats):
39	                return True
40	    return False
41

43	def convert_dataset(path: str):
44	    raw_dataset = load_dataset(path)
45	    dataset = []
46	    for data in raw_dataset["train"]:
47	        messages = data["prompt"]
48	        schema = data["schema"]
49	        obj = json.loads(schema)
50	
51	        # skip some corrupted examples
52	        if obj.get("type", None) is None:
53	            continue
54	
55	        # skip schema with format "email"
56	        # which is not supported by outlines for now
57	        if contains_formats(obj, ["email"]):
58	            continue
59	
60	        system = messages[0]
61	        user = messages[1]
62	        assert system["role"] == "system", "invalid role"
63	        assert user["role"] == "user", "invalid role"
64	        assert len(messages) == 2, "invalid message length"
65	        message = json.dumps(system["content"]), json.dumps(user["content"])
66	        dataset.append(
67	            {
68	                "message": message,
69	                "json_schema": schema,
70	            }
71	        )
72	
73	    return dataset

775	    def _resolve_sampling_params(self, sampling_params):
776	        """
777	        Construct sampling param based on default + override values
778	
779	        The default values of sampling are populated in `default_sampling_para` via sgl.function.run(...sampling_args)
780	        , and `sampling_params` contains the override values from sgl.gen().
781	
782	        Here we use default_sampling_para as the base and override the values if they exist in `sampling_params`.
783	        It also extends the stop tokens based on the chat template.
784	        """
785	
786	        # deepcopy is required because the dict has lists inside
787	        clone = copy.deepcopy(self.default_sampling_para)
788	
789	        for item in [
790	            "max_new_tokens",
791	            "min_new_tokens",
792	            "n",
793	            "stop",
794	            "stop_token_ids",
795	            "stop_regex",
796	            "temperature",
797	            "top_p",
798	            "top_k",
799	            "min_p",
800	            "frequency_penalty",
801	            "presence_penalty",
802	            "ignore_eos",
803	            "return_logprob",
804	            "logprob_start_len",
805	            "top_logprobs_num",
806	            "return_text_in_logprobs",
807	            "dtype",
808	            "regex",
809	            "json_schema",
810	        ]:
811	            value = getattr(sampling_params, item, None)
812	            if value is not None:
813	                setattr(clone, item, value)
814	
815	        if self.chat_template.stop_str:
816	            if clone.stop == ():
817	                clone.stop = []
818	            elif isinstance(clone.stop, str):
819	                clone.stop = [clone.stop]
820	            clone.stop += self.chat_template.stop_str
821	
822	        return clone

127	    def _handle_dtype_to_regex(self, sampling_params: SglSamplingParams):
128	        if sampling_params.dtype is None:
129	            return
130	
131	        if sampling_params.stop == ():
132	            sampling_params.stop = []
133	
134	        dtype_regex = None
135	        if sampling_params.dtype in ["int", int]:
136	
137	            dtype_regex = REGEX_INT
138	            sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
139	        elif sampling_params.dtype in ["float", float]:
140	
141	            dtype_regex = REGEX_FLOAT
142	            sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
143	        elif sampling_params.dtype in ["str", str]:
144	
145	            dtype_regex = REGEX_STR
146	        elif sampling_params.dtype in ["bool", bool]:
147	
148	            dtype_regex = REGEX_BOOL
149	        else:
150	            raise RuntimeError(f"Invalid dtype: {sampling_params.dtype}")
151	
152	        if dtype_regex is not None and sampling_params.regex is not None:
153	            warnings.warn(
154	                f"Both dtype and regex are set. Only dtype will be used. dtype: {sampling_params.dtype}, regex: {sampling_params.regex}"
155	            )
156	
157	        sampling_params.regex = dtype_regex
158

186	        res = http_request(

188	            json=data,
189	            api_key=self.api_key,
190	            verify=self.verify,
191	        )
192	        self._assert_success(res)
193	
194	        obj = res.json()
195	        comp = obj["text"]
196	        return comp, obj["meta_info"]

23	        s += "A: Berlin\n"
24	        s += "Q: What is the capital of Italy?\n"
25	        s += "A: Rome\n"

28	
29	    ret = few_shot_qa.run(question="What is the capital of the United States?")
30	    assert "washington" in ret["answer"].strip().lower(), f"answer: {ret['answer']}"
31	
32	    rets = few_shot_qa.run_batch(
33	        [
34	            {"question": "What is the capital of Japan?"},
35	            {"question": "What is the capital of the United Kingdom?"},
36	            {"question": "What is the capital city of China?"},
37	        ],
38	        temperature=0.1,
39	    )
40	    answers = [x["answer"].strip().lower() for x in rets]

input.description: Explore SGLang backend and grammar constraint integration
input.prompt:
I need to understand how SGLang's backend applies grammar/format constraints during LLM generation.

Please explore the codebase to find:
1. The backend server code that handles generation requests
2. How structured output / constrained decoding is implemented (look for outlines, lm-format-enforcer, or xgrammar integrations)
3. The request/response objects that carry constraint information
4. How JSON schema validation is done
5. Any grammar or finite-state machine based constraint enforcement
6. Files in directories like: `sglang/srt/`, `sglang/lang/`, `sglang/backend/`

Search for: "grammar", "outlines", "lm_format_enforcer", "xgrammar", "json_schema", "constrained", "GrammarConstraint", "StructuredOutput"

Be thorough - read the relevant source files and trace the data flow. Report file paths, class names, and key implementation details.

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed report.

## SGLang Output Constraints System - Comprehensive Analysis

Based on my thorough exploration of the SGLang codebase, here's a complete overview of how output constraints work:

### 1. EXISTING OUTPUT CONSTRAINT TYPES

SGLang supports four main constraint types:

1. **JSON Schema** (`json_schema`): Constrains output to match a JSON schema
2. **Regex** (`regex`): Constrains output to match a regular expression pattern
3. **EBNF** (`ebnf`): Constrains output using Extended Backus-Naur Form grammar
4. **Structural Tag** (`structural_tag`): A complex constraint for structured outputs with tags

#### Constraint Type Hierarchy
- All constraints are **mutually exclusive** - only one can be used per generation request
- Validation happens in `SamplingParams.verify()` at `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py` line 155-161

### 2. FILES WHERE CONSTRAINTS ARE DEFINED AND REGISTERED

#### Core Constraint Framework Files:

**Base Classes & Manager:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py`
  - `BaseGrammarObject`: Abstract base class for all grammar objects
  - `BaseGrammarBackend`: Abstract base class for all grammar backends
  - `GrammarStats`: Tracks compilation statistics
  - `create_grammar_backend()`: Factory function to instantiate backends
  - `GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY`: Dict for registering custom backends
  
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py`
  - `GrammarManager`: Manages grammar preprocessing and queueing

**Sampling Parameters:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py`
  - `SamplingParams` class: Defines all constraint fields (`json_schema`, `regex`, `ebnf`, `structural_tag`)

**Language API:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/lang/api.py`
  - `gen()`: Main function accepting `regex` and `json_schema` parameters
  - `gen_int()`, `gen_string()`: Specialized generators
  - `select()`: Choice-based generation
  
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/lang/ir.py`
  - `SglGen`: IR class that wraps SamplingParams with constraints

#### Backend Implementations (Grammar Backends):

Three pluggable backend implementations:

1. **Outlines Backend**
   - File: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/srt/constrained/outlines_backend.py`
   - Classes: `OutlinesGrammar`, `OutlinesGrammarBackend`
   - Dispatch Methods:
     - `dispatch_json(key_string)`: Converts JSON schema to regex via `build_regex_from_schema()`, then uses Outlines RegexGuide
     - `dispatch_regex(key_string)`: Creates RegexGuide from regex pattern
     - `dispatch_ebnf(key_string)`: Not supported (delegates to parent)
     - `dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)`: Not supported (delegates to parent)
   - Jump Forward Support: Yes (via `OutlinesJumpForwardMap`)

2. **XGrammar Backend**
   - File: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/srt/constrained/xgrammar_backend.py`
   - Classes: `XGrammarGrammar`, `XGrammarGrammarBackend`
   - Dispatch Methods:
     - `dispatch_json(key_string)`: Uses `GrammarCompiler.compile_json_schema()`
     - `dispatch_regex(key_string)`: Uses `GrammarCompiler.compile_regex()`
     - `dispatch_ebnf(key_string)`: Uses `GrammarCompiler.compile_grammar()`
     - `dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)`: Handles both legacy and new format structural tags
   - Jump Forward Support: Yes (via GrammarMatcher)
   - Special Features: Tokenizer support validation, rollback mechanism (up to 200 tokens)

3. **LLGuidance Backend**
   - File: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/srt/constrained/llguidance_backend.py`
   - Classes: `GuidanceGrammar`, `GuidanceBackend`
   - Dispatch Methods:
     - `dispatch_json(key_string)`: Uses `LLMatcher.grammar_from_json_schema()`
     - `dispatch_regex(key_string)`: Uses `grammar_from("regex", key_string)`
     - `dispatch_ebnf(key_string)`: Uses `grammar_from("ebnf", key_string)`
     - `dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)`: Uses `StructTag` with legacy format handling
   - Jump Forward Support: Yes (via `compute_ff_tokens()`)

#### Optimization Layer:
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/srt/constrained/outlines_jump_forward.py`
  - Implements "Jump Forward Decoding" optimization
  - Compresses FSM for faster matching
  - References: https://lmsys.org/blog/2024-02-05-compressed-fsm/

#### Reasoner (Extended Thinking) Integration:
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/srt/constrained/reasoner_grammar_backend.py`
  - `ReasonerGrammarObject`: Wraps another grammar backend
  - Tracks thinking state separately from grammar constraint
  - Only applies constraints after thinking phase ends

### 3. CONSTRAINT EXECUTION PIPELINE

The constraint execution pipeline follows these steps:

#### Step 1: User-Facing API
```python
sgl.gen(regex=pattern, json_schema=schema, ebnf=grammar)
```

#### Step 2: IR Creation
- Creates `SglGen` object with `SglSamplingParams`
- Parameters stored in request object

#### Step 3: Grammar Manager Queue
- `GrammarManager.process_req_with_grammar()` checks if any constraint is set
- Creates cache key: `(type, constraint_string)` where type is "json", "regex", "ebnf", or "structural_tag"
- Checks backend cache for compiled grammar
- If not cached, submits to thread pool for async compilation

#### Step 4: Backend Dispatch (Compilation)
- Backend's `dispatch_*()` method converts constraint to grammar object
- Each backend has its own compilation strategy
- Returns `BaseGrammarObject` instance (or `INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ` on error)
- Results are cached for reuse

#### Step 5: Batch Info Setup
- `SamplingBatchInfo.from_schedule_batch()` collects all grammars in batch
- Creates list of `BaseGrammarObject` instances for batch

#### Step 6: Vocab Mask Creation
- `SamplingBatchInfo.update_regex_vocab_mask()` called during model execution
- For each grammar in batch:
  - Allocates vocab mask tensor via `grammar.allocate_vocab_mask()`
  - Fills mask via `grammar.fill_vocab_mask()` (marks allowed tokens as True)
  - Moves mask to device via `grammar.move_vocab_mask()`

#### Step 7: Token Generation Loop
```
for each generation step:
  1. Model generates logits
  2. SamplingBatchInfo.apply_logits_bias() applies mask:
     - Calls grammar.apply_vocab_mask(logits, vocab_mask)
     - Sets logits to -inf for disallowed tokens
  3. Sample tokens from masked logits
  4. For each token:
     - grammar.accept_token(token_id)
     - Updates grammar state for next iteration
  5. Check termination via grammar.is_terminated()
```

#### Step 8: Optimization (Jump Forward)
- Grammar may provide jump-forward optimization
- `grammar.try_jump_forward(tokenizer)` returns multi-token sequences to accept
- `grammar.jump_forward_str_state()` gets next string and state
- `grammar.jump_and_retokenize()` updates grammar state after jump

### 4. BASE CLASSES AND INTERFACES

#### BaseGrammarObject (Abstract)
Location: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py` (Line 42)

Key methods all implementations must provide:
```python
class BaseGrammarObject:
    def accept_token(self, token: int) -> None
        """Accept a token and update grammar state."""
        
    def rollback(self, k: int) -> None
        """Rollback the last k tokens."""
        
    def is_terminated(self) -> bool
        """Check if grammar has reached terminal state."""
        
    def allocate_vocab_mask(self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device) -> torch.Tensor
        """Allocate vocab mask tensor (batch_size, vocab_size) bool tensor."""
        
    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None
        """Fill vocab mask for request idx (marks invalid tokens as True)."""
        
    @staticmethod
    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor
        """Move vocab mask to target device."""
        
    @staticmethod
    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None
        """Apply mask to logits (set masked positions to -inf)."""
        
    def copy(self) -> "BaseGrammarObject"
        """Create a copy for batch reuse."""
        
    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]
        """Try to jump multiple tokens at once (optimization)."""
        
    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]
        """Get next state after jump."""
        
    def jump_and_retokenize(self, old_output_ids, new_output_ids, next_state) -> None
        """Update grammar state after jump+retokenization."""
```

#### BaseGrammarBackend (Abstract)
Location: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py` (Line 129)

Key methods:

```python
class BaseGrammarBackend:
    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
    def dispatch_fallback(self, key_type: str, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
    def get_cached_or_future_value(self, key, require_reasoning) -> Tuple[BaseGrammarObject, bool]
    def reset(self)
```

### 5. USER-FACING API EXAMPLES

#### Generation with Constraints

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/lang/api.py` (Lines 75-139)

```python
# JSON Schema Generation
sgl.gen(
    name="output",
    json_schema=json.dumps({
        "type": "object",
        "properties": {
            "name": {"type": "string"},
            "age": {"type": "integer"}
        }
    })
)

# Regex Generation

    name="output",
    regex=r"^\d{3}-\d{3}-\d{4}$"  # Phone number
)

# EBNF Grammar Generation

    name="output",
    ebnf="""
    root ::= greeting name
    greeting ::= "Hello" | "Hi"
    name ::= [a-z]+
    """
)

# With other sampling params
sgl.gen(
    max_tokens=100,
    temperature=0.7,
    top_p=0.9,
    stop=["."],
    regex=r"^[a-zA-Z\s]+$",
    return_logprob=True

```

#### Choice Selection API

```python
    name="category",
    choices=["A", "B", "C"],
    choices_method=sgl.token_length_normalized

```

#### OpenAI-Compatible API
```python

    model="model_name",
    messages=[...],
    response_format={
        "type": "json_schema",
        "json_schema": {
            "name": "output_schema",
            "schema": {...}
        }
```

### 6. CONSTRAINT APPLICATION DURING GENERATION

**Key File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_batch_info.py`

The constraint application happens in two critical methods:

#### Vocab Mask Update
```python
def update_regex_vocab_mask(self):
    """Called before each generation step"""
    if not self.grammars:
        return
    
    # Get first grammar to determine mask type/backend
    first_grammar = next(grammar for grammar in self.grammars if grammar)
    
    # Allocate batch vocab mask
    self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.allocate_vocab_mask(
        vocab_size=self.vocab_size,
        batch_size=len(self.temperatures),
        device=self.device
    )
    
    # Fill masks for each request
    for i, grammar in enumerate(self.grammars):
        if grammar and not grammar.finished and not grammar.is_terminated():
            grammar.fill_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, i)
    
    # Move to device
    self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.move_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, self.device)
```

#### Logits Masking
```python
def apply_logits_bias(self, logits: torch.Tensor):
    """Apply all biases/constraints to logits"""
    # ... penalties and custom logits applied first ...
    
    if self.vocab_mask is not None:
        # Apply grammar constraint
        self.apply_mask_func(logits=logits, vocab_mask=self.vocab_mask)
    
    # ... token biases applied last ...
```

**Model Integration** (`model_runner.py`):
```python
# In forward pass
sampling_info.update_regex_vocab_mask()  # Update masks
sampling_info.apply_logits_bias(logits_output.next_token_logits)  # Apply constraints
```

```python
class GrammarStats:
    compilation_time: Optional[float] = None  # Time to compile grammar
    schema_count: Optional[int] = None        # Number of schemas
    ebnf_size: Optional[int] = None           # EBNF grammar size in chars
    is_cache_hit: bool = False                # Cached compilation?
    is_grammar_aborted: bool = False          # Aborted due to timeout?
    tree_traversal_time: List[float] = []     # Per-step traversal times
    dispatch_type: Optional[str] = None       # "json", "regex", "ebnf", "structural_tag"
    num_timeout: int = 0                      # Timeout count
```

#### Constraint Key Format
All constraints are indexed by: `(constraint_type: str, constraint_value: str)`

**Types**:
- `"json"`: JSON schema string
- `"regex"`: Regex pattern string
- `"ebnf"`: EBNF grammar string
- `"structural_tag"`: Structural tag JSON string
- `"structural_pattern"`: Structural pattern (new format)
- `"structural_pattern_v2"`: Structural pattern v2 (new format)

### 9. ERROR HANDLING AND VALIDATION

**Validation** (sampling_params.py):
- Only one constraint can be set (mutually exclusive)
- Regex syntax validated via `re.compile()`
- Max sequence length calculated for stop_regex

**Backend Fallback**:
- If constraint compilation fails: return `INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ`
- Errors logged but don't crash generation
- Request continues without constraint if grammar_backend="none"

**Timeout Handling** (grammar_manager.py):
- Grammar compilation happens in thread pool
- Polling with configurable timeout (env: `SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL`)
- Requests timeout after max iterations (env: `SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS`)

### 10. PERFORMANCE OPTIMIZATIONS

1. **Caching**: Compiled grammars cached by `(type, value)` key
2. **Jump Forward Decoding**: Multi-token acceptance for patterns with deterministic sequences
3. **Lazy Compilation**: Constraints compiled asynchronously in thread pool
4. **Bitmask Operations**: GPU-accelerated vocab mask application via Triton/HIP
5. **Batch Optimization**: Masks computed once per batch, reused for all batch items

---

This comprehensive system enables SGLang to enforce structured outputs while maintaining generation speed through careful optimization and multi-backend support.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed report.

## SGLang Output Constraints System - Comprehensive Analysis

Based on my thorough exploration of the SGLang codebase, here's a complete overview of how output constraints work:

### 1. EXISTING OUTPUT CONSTRAINT TYPES

SGLang supports four main constraint types:

1. **JSON Schema** (`json_schema`): Constrains output to match a JSON schema
2. **Regex** (`regex`): Constrains output to match a regular expression pattern
3. **EBNF** (`ebnf`): Constrains output using Extended Backus-Naur Form grammar
4. **Structural Tag** (`structural_tag`): A complex constraint for structured outputs with tags

#### Constraint Type Hierarchy
- All constraints are **mutually exclusive** - only one can be used per generation request
- Validation happens in `SamplingParams.verify()` at `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py` line 155-161

### 2. FILES WHERE CONSTRAINTS ARE DEFINED AND REGISTERED

#### Core Constraint Framework Files:

**Base Classes & Manager:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py`
  - `BaseGrammarObject`: Abstract base class for all grammar objects
  - `BaseGrammarBackend`: Abstract base class for all grammar backends
  - `GrammarStats`: Tracks compilation statistics
  - `create_grammar_backend()`: Factory function to instantiate backends
  - `GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY`: Dict for registering custom backends
  
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py`
  - `GrammarManager`: Manages grammar preprocessing and queueing

**Sampling Parameters:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py`
  - `SamplingParams` class: Defines all constraint fields (`json_schema`, `regex`, `ebnf`, `structural_tag`)

**Language API:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/lang/api.py`
  - `gen()`: Main function accepting `regex` and `json_schema` parameters
  - `gen_int()`, `gen_string()`: Specialized generators
  - `select()`: Choice-based generation
  
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/lang/ir.py`
  - `SglGen`: IR class that wraps SamplingParams with constraints

#### Backend Implementations (Grammar Backends):

Three pluggable backend implementations:

1. **Outlines Backend**
   - File: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/srt/constrained/outlines_backend.py`
   - Classes: `OutlinesGrammar`, `OutlinesGrammarBackend`
   - Dispatch Methods:
     - `dispatch_json(key_string)`: Converts JSON schema to regex via `build_regex_from_schema()`, then uses Outlines RegexGuide
     - `dispatch_regex(key_string)`: Creates RegexGuide from regex pattern
     - `dispatch_ebnf(key_string)`: Not supported (delegates to parent)
     - `dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)`: Not supported (delegates to parent)
   - Jump Forward Support: Yes (via `OutlinesJumpForwardMap`)

2. **XGrammar Backend**
   - File: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/srt/constrained/xgrammar_backend.py`
   - Classes: `XGrammarGrammar`, `XGrammarGrammarBackend`
   - Dispatch Methods:
     - `dispatch_json(key_string)`: Uses `GrammarCompiler.compile_json_schema()`
     - `dispatch_regex(key_string)`: Uses `GrammarCompiler.compile_regex()`
     - `dispatch_ebnf(key_string)`: Uses `GrammarCompiler.compile_grammar()`
     - `dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)`: Handles both legacy and new format structural tags
   - Jump Forward Support: Yes (via GrammarMatcher)
   - Special Features: Tokenizer support validation, rollback mechanism (up to 200 tokens)

3. **LLGuidance Backend**
   - File: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/srt/constrained/llguidance_backend.py`
   - Classes: `GuidanceGrammar`, `GuidanceBackend`
   - Dispatch Methods:
     - `dispatch_json(key_string)`: Uses `LLMatcher.grammar_from_json_schema()`
     - `dispatch_regex(key_string)`: Uses `grammar_from("regex", key_string)`
     - `dispatch_ebnf(key_string)`: Uses `grammar_from("ebnf", key_string)`
     - `dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)`: Uses `StructTag` with legacy format handling
   - Jump Forward Support: Yes (via `compute_ff_tokens()`)

#### Optimization Layer:
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/srt/constrained/outlines_jump_forward.py`
  - Implements "Jump Forward Decoding" optimization
  - Compresses FSM for faster matching
  - References: https://lmsys.org/blog/2024-02-05-compressed-fsm/

#### Reasoner (Extended Thinking) Integration:
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/srt/constrained/reasoner_grammar_backend.py`
  - `ReasonerGrammarObject`: Wraps another grammar backend
  - Tracks thinking state separately from grammar constraint
  - Only applies constraints after thinking phase ends

### 3. CONSTRAINT EXECUTION PIPELINE

The constraint execution pipeline follows these steps:

#### Step 1: User-Facing API
```python
sgl.gen(regex=pattern, json_schema=schema, ebnf=grammar)
```

#### Step 2: IR Creation
- Creates `SglGen` object with `SglSamplingParams`
- Parameters stored in request object

#### Step 3: Grammar Manager Queue
- `GrammarManager.process_req_with_grammar()` checks if any constraint is set
- Creates cache key: `(type, constraint_string)` where type is "json", "regex", "ebnf", or "structural_tag"
- Checks backend cache for compiled grammar
- If not cached, submits to thread pool for async compilation

#### Step 4: Backend Dispatch (Compilation)
- Backend's `dispatch_*()` method converts constraint to grammar object
- Each backend has its own compilation strategy
- Returns `BaseGrammarObject` instance (or `INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ` on error)
- Results are cached for reuse

#### Step 5: Batch Info Setup
- `SamplingBatchInfo.from_schedule_batch()` collects all grammars in batch
- Creates list of `BaseGrammarObject` instances for batch

#### Step 6: Vocab Mask Creation
- `SamplingBatchInfo.update_regex_vocab_mask()` called during model execution
- For each grammar in batch:
  - Allocates vocab mask tensor via `grammar.allocate_vocab_mask()`
  - Fills mask via `grammar.fill_vocab_mask()` (marks allowed tokens as True)
  - Moves mask to device via `grammar.move_vocab_mask()`

#### Step 7: Token Generation Loop
```
for each generation step:
  1. Model generates logits
  2. SamplingBatchInfo.apply_logits_bias() applies mask:
     - Calls grammar.apply_vocab_mask(logits, vocab_mask)
     - Sets logits to -inf for disallowed tokens
  3. Sample tokens from masked logits
  4. For each token:
     - grammar.accept_token(token_id)
     - Updates grammar state for next iteration
  5. Check termination via grammar.is_terminated()
```

#### Step 8: Optimization (Jump Forward)
- Grammar may provide jump-forward optimization
- `grammar.try_jump_forward(tokenizer)` returns multi-token sequences to accept
- `grammar.jump_forward_str_state()` gets next string and state
- `grammar.jump_and_retokenize()` updates grammar state after jump

### 4. BASE CLASSES AND INTERFACES

#### BaseGrammarObject (Abstract)
Location: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py` (Line 42)

Key methods all implementations must provide:
```python
class BaseGrammarObject:
    def accept_token(self, token: int) -> None
        """Accept a token and update grammar state."""
        
    def rollback(self, k: int) -> None
        """Rollback the last k tokens."""
        
    def is_terminated(self) -> bool
        """Check if grammar has reached terminal state."""
        
    def allocate_vocab_mask(self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device) -> torch.Tensor
        """Allocate vocab mask tensor (batch_size, vocab_size) bool tensor."""
        
    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None
        """Fill vocab mask for request idx (marks invalid tokens as True)."""
        
    @staticmethod
    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor
        """Move vocab mask to target device."""
        
    @staticmethod
    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None
        """Apply mask to logits (set masked positions to -inf)."""
        
    def copy(self) -> "BaseGrammarObject"
        """Create a copy for batch reuse."""
        
    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]
        """Try to jump multiple tokens at once (optimization)."""
        
    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]
        """Get next state after jump."""
        
    def jump_and_retokenize(self, old_output_ids, new_output_ids, next_state) -> None
        """Update grammar state after jump+retokenization."""
```

#### BaseGrammarBackend (Abstract)
Location: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py` (Line 129)

Key methods:
```python
class BaseGrammarBackend:
    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
    def dispatch_fallback(self, key_type: str, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
    def get_cached_or_future_value(self, key, require_reasoning) -> Tuple[BaseGrammarObject, bool]
    def reset(self)
```

### 5. USER-FACING API EXAMPLES

#### Generation with Constraints

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/lang/api.py` (Lines 75-139)

```python
# JSON Schema Generation
sgl.gen(
    name="output",
    json_schema=json.dumps({
        "type": "object",
        "properties": {
            "name": {"type": "string"},
            "age": {"type": "integer"}
        }
    })
)

# Regex Generation
sgl.gen(
    name="output",
    regex=r"^\d{3}-\d{3}-\d{4}$"  # Phone number
)

# EBNF Grammar Generation
sgl.gen(
    name="output",
    ebnf="""
    root ::= greeting name
    greeting ::= "Hello" | "Hi"
    name ::= [a-z]+
    """
)

# With other sampling params
sgl.gen(
    max_tokens=100,
    temperature=0.7,
    top_p=0.9,
    stop=["."],
    regex=r"^[a-zA-Z\s]+$",
    return_logprob=True
)
```

#### Choice Selection API
```python
sgl.select(
    name="category",
    choices=["A", "B", "C"],
    choices_method=sgl.token_length_normalized
)
```

#### OpenAI-Compatible API
```python
# Via OpenAI SDK
response = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="model_name",
    messages=[...],
    response_format={
        "type": "json_schema",
        "json_schema": {
            "name": "output_schema",
            "schema": {...}
        }
    }
)
```

### 6. CONSTRAINT APPLICATION DURING GENERATION

**Key File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_batch_info.py`

The constraint application happens in two critical methods:

#### Vocab Mask Update
```python
def update_regex_vocab_mask(self):
    """Called before each generation step"""
    if not self.grammars:
        return
    
    # Get first grammar to determine mask type/backend
    first_grammar = next(grammar for grammar in self.grammars if grammar)
    
    # Allocate batch vocab mask
    self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.allocate_vocab_mask(
        vocab_size=self.vocab_size,
        batch_size=len(self.temperatures),
        device=self.device
    )
    
    # Fill masks for each request
    for i, grammar in enumerate(self.grammars):
        if grammar and not grammar.finished and not grammar.is_terminated():
            grammar.fill_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, i)
    
    # Move to device
    self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.move_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, self.device)
```

#### Logits Masking
```python
def apply_logits_bias(self, logits: torch.Tensor):
    """Apply all biases/constraints to logits"""
    # ... penalties and custom logits applied first ...
    
    if self.vocab_mask is not None:
        # Apply grammar constraint
        self.apply_mask_func(logits=logits, vocab_mask=self.vocab_mask)
    
    # ... token biases applied last ...
```

**Model Integration** (`model_runner.py`):
```python
# In forward pass
sampling_info.update_regex_vocab_mask()  # Update masks
sampling_info.apply_logits_bias(logits_output.next_token_logits)  # Apply constraints
```

### 7. PRACTICAL TEST EXAMPLES

```python
}

# Send to server

    base_url + "/generate",
    json={
        "text": "Introduce the capital of France. Return in JSON format.",
        "sampling_params": {
            "temperature": 0,
            "max_new_tokens": 128,
            "json_schema": json_schema

```

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/test/kits/regex_constrained_kit.py`

```python

    base_url + "/generate",
    json={
        "text": "Generate an email address:",
        "sampling_params": {
            "regex": r"^user@example\.com$",
            "max_new_tokens": 128
```

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/test/kits/ebnf_constrained_kit.py`

```python
area ::= [0-9] [0-9] [0-9]

    base_url + "/generate",

        "text": "Generate a phone number:",
```

### 8. KEY STATISTICS AND DATA STRUCTURES

**Location**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py` (Line 31)

11	    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,

16	if TYPE_CHECKING:
17	    from sglang.srt.managers.io_struct import AbortReq
18	    from sglang.srt.managers.schedule_batch import Req
19	    from sglang.srt.managers.scheduler import Scheduler

24	class GrammarManager:
25	    def __init__(self, scheduler: Scheduler):
26	        self.scheduler = scheduler
27	        self.server_args = scheduler.server_args
28	        self.grammar_queue: List[Req] = []
29	        if not self.server_args.skip_tokenizer_init:
30	            self.grammar_backend = create_grammar_backend(
31	                self.server_args,
32	                scheduler.tokenizer,
33	                scheduler.model_config.vocab_size,
34	                scheduler.model_config.hf_eos_token_id,
35	            )
36	        else:
37	            self.grammar_backend = None
38	
39	        self.grammar_sync_group = scheduler.dp_tp_cpu_group
40	        self.grammar_sync_size = scheduler.dp_tp_group.world_size
41	        self.grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.first_rank
42	        self.is_grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.is_first_rank
43	
44	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL = envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL.get()
45	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS = (
46	            envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS.get()
47	        )
48	
49	    def __len__(self):
50	        return len(self.grammar_queue)
51	
52	    def clear(self):
53	        if self.grammar_backend:
54	            self.grammar_backend.reset()
55	
56	    def has_waiting_grammars(self) -> bool:
57	        return len(self.grammar_queue) > 0
58	
59	    def abort_requests(self, recv_req: AbortReq):
60	        for req in self.grammar_queue:
61	            if recv_req.abort_all or req.rid.startswith(recv_req.rid):
62	                logger.debug(f"Abort grammar queue request. {req.rid=}")
63	                if req.grammar:
64	                    req.grammar.cancel()
65	                req.set_finish_with_abort("Aborted by AbortReq.")
66	
67	    def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
68	        # Init grammar cache for this request
69	        add_to_grammar_queue = False
70	        if (
71	            req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
72	            or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
73	            or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
74	            or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
75	        ):
76	            if self.grammar_backend is None:
77	                error_msg = "Grammar-based generation (json_schema, regex, ebnf, structural_tag) is not supported when the server is launched with --grammar-backend none"
78	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
79	            else:
80	                if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
81	                    key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
82	                elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
83	                    key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
84	                elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
85	                    key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
86	                elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
87	                    key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
88	
89	                value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
90	                    key, req.require_reasoning
91	                )
92	                req.grammar = value
93	
94	                if not cache_hit:
95	                    req.grammar_key = key
96	                    add_to_grammar_queue = True
97	                else:
98	                    if value is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:  # We hit a cached invalid grammar.
99	                        error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request with cache hit: {key=}"
100	                        req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
101	
102	        if add_to_grammar_queue:
103	            self.grammar_queue.append(req)
104	
105	        return add_to_grammar_queue
106	
107	    def get_ready_grammar_requests(self) -> List[Req]:
108	        """
109	        Move requests whose grammar objects are ready from grammar_queue to waiting_queue.
110	
111	        Rank i returns two sets ready_reqs_i, failed_reqs_i
112	        ready_reqs_all = all_gather(ready_reqs_i)
113	        failed_reqs_all = all_gather(failed_reqs_i)
114	
115	        ready_reqs = intersect(ready_reqs_all)
116	        failed_reqs = union(failed_reqs_all)
117	        """
118	        ready_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
119	        failed_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
120	
121	        # Poll for ready requests
122	        start_time = time.perf_counter()
123	        while time.perf_counter() - start_time < self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL:
124	            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
125	                if i in ready_req_idxs:
126	                    continue
127	
128	                if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
129	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
130	                    continue
131	
132	                assert isinstance(req.grammar, futures.Future), f"{req=}"
133	                if req.grammar.done():
134	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
135	
136	            # Sleep a bit to avoid busy waiting
137	            time.sleep(self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL / 10)
138	
139	        # Check failed requests
140	        for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
141	            if i not in ready_req_idxs:
142	                self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct += 1
143	                if (
144	                    self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct
145	                    >= self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS
146	                ):
147	                    # Timeout after max poll iterations
148	                    # The actual waiting time is SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS * max(SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL, GPU_forward_batch_latency)
149	                    failed_req_idxs.add(i)
150	
151	        # Sync ready and failed requests across all ranks
152	        if self.grammar_sync_size == 1:
153	            synced_ready_req_idxs = ready_req_idxs
154	            synced_failed_req_idxs = failed_req_idxs
155	        else:
156	            all_gather_output = [None] * self.grammar_sync_size
157	            torch.distributed.all_gather_object(
158	                all_gather_output,
159	                (ready_req_idxs, failed_req_idxs),
160	                group=self.grammar_sync_group,
161	            )
162	            synced_ready_req_idxs = set.intersection(*[x[0] for x in all_gather_output])
163	            synced_failed_req_idxs = set.union(*[x[1] for x in all_gather_output])
164	
165	        # Return ready requests
166	        return_reqs: List[Req] = []
167	        for i in synced_ready_req_idxs:
168	            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
169	            return_reqs.append(req)
170	            if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
171	                continue
172	
173	            req.grammar = req.grammar.result()
174	            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, req.grammar.copy())
175	            if req.grammar is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:
176	                error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request: {req.grammar_key=}"
177	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
178	
179	        # Return failed requests
180	        for i in synced_failed_req_idxs:
181	            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
182	            return_reqs.append(req)
183	
184	            req.grammar.cancel()
185	            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ)
186	            error_msg = f"Grammar preprocessing timed out: {req.grammar_key=}"
187	            req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
188	
189	        # Remove finished requests from grammar_queue
190	        self.grammar_queue = [
191	            req
192	            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue)
193	            if i not in synced_ready_req_idxs and i not in synced_failed_req_idxs
194	        ]
195	        return return_reqs
196

18	from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
19	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
20	from threading import Event
21	from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Tuple

30	@dataclass
31	class GrammarStats:
32	    compilation_time: Optional[float] = None
33	    schema_count: Optional[int] = None
34	    ebnf_size: Optional[int] = None
35	    is_cache_hit: bool = False
36	    is_grammar_aborted: bool = False
37	    tree_traversal_time: List[float] = field(default_factory=list)
38	    dispatch_type: Optional[str] = None
39	    num_timeout: int = 0

42	class BaseGrammarObject:
43	
44	    def __init__(self):
45	        self._finished = False
46	        self.grammar_stats = None
47	        self.current_token = None
48	
49	    def maybe_init_reasoning(self, reasoning: bool):
50	        pass
51	
52	    def accept_token(self, token: int) -> None:
53	        """
54	        Accept a token in the grammar.
55	        """
56	        raise NotImplementedError()
57	
58	    def rollback(self, k: int):
59	        raise NotImplementedError()
60	
61	    def is_terminated(self):
62	        return False
63	
64	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
65	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
66	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
67	        raise NotImplementedError()
68	
69	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
70	        raise NotImplementedError()
71	
72	    @staticmethod
73	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
74	        raise NotImplementedError()
75	
76	    @staticmethod
77	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
78	        raise NotImplementedError()
79	
80	    def copy(self) -> "BaseGrammarObject":
81	        return self
82	
83	    @property
84	    def finished(self):
85	        return self._finished
86	
87	    @finished.setter
88	    def finished(self, finished):
89	        self._finished = finished
90	
91	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]:
92	        """
93	        Try to jump forward in the grammar.
94	
95	        Returns:
96	            A jump forward helper which may be used in `jump_forward_str_state`.
97	            None if the jump forward is not possible.
98	        """
99	        raise NotImplementedError()

101	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
102	        """
103	        Jump forward for the grammar.
104	
105	        Returns:
106	            A tuple of the jump forward string and the next state of the grammar
107	            (which can be used in `jump_and_retokenize` if needed).
108	        """
109	        raise NotImplementedError()
110	
111	    def jump_and_retokenize(
112	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
113	    ) -> None:
114	        """
115	        Jump forward occurs, and update the grammar state if needed.
116	        """
117	        raise NotImplementedError()

120	INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ = BaseGrammarObject()

123	@dataclass
124	class CacheEntry:
125	    value: BaseGrammarObject
126	    event: Event
127	
128	
129	class BaseGrammarBackend:
130	    def __init__(self):
131	        self.executor = ThreadPoolExecutor()
132	        self.cache: Dict[Tuple[str, str], CacheEntry] = {}
133	
134	    def _not_supported(self, key_type: str, key_string: str) -> None:
135	        logger.warning(f"Skip unsupported {key_type=}, {key_string=}")
136	
137	    def dispatch_fallback(
138	        self, key_type: str, key_string: str
139	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
140	        """
141	        This function should not be reached in any case.
142	        """
143	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid key_type: {key_type}={key_string}")
144	
145	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
146	        return self._not_supported("json", key_string)
147	
148	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
149	        return self._not_supported("regex", key_string)
150	
151	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
152	        return self._not_supported("ebnf", key_string)
153	
154	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
155	        return self._not_supported("structural_tag", key_string)
156	
157	    def _init_value_dispatch(
158	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
159	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
160	        s = time.perf_counter()
161	        key_type, key_string = key
162	        if key_type == "json":
163	            grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
164	        elif key_type == "regex":
165	            grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
166	        elif key_type == "ebnf":
167	            grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
168	        elif key_type == "structural_tag":
169	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
170	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern":
171	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern(key_string)
172	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern_v2":
173	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern_v2(key_string)
174	        else:
175	            grammar = self.dispatch_fallback(key_type, key_string)
176	
177	        if grammar is not None and grammar.grammar_stats is not None:
178	            grammar.grammar_stats.compilation_time = time.perf_counter() - s
179	        return grammar
180	
181	    def get_cached_or_future_value(
182	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
183	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
184	        value = self.cache.get(key)
185	        if value:
186	            copied_value = value.copy()
187	            copied_value.maybe_init_reasoning(require_reasoning)
188	            return copied_value, True
189	        value = self.executor.submit(self._init_value_dispatch, key, require_reasoning)
190	        return value, False
191	
192	    def set_cache(self, key: Tuple[str, str], value: BaseGrammarObject):
193	        self.cache[key] = value
194	
195	    def reset(self):
196	        self.cache.clear()
197

199	GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY = {}
200	
201	
202	def register_grammar_backend(name, init_func):
203	    GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name] = init_func
204	
205	
206	def create_grammar_backend(
207	    server_args: ServerArgs,
208	    tokenizer,
209	    vocab_size: int,
210	    eos_token_ids: Optional[set] = None,
211	) -> Optional[BaseGrammarBackend]:
212	    name = server_args.grammar_backend
213	
214	    # Custom grammar backend has the highest priority
215	    if name in GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY:
216	        return GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name](
217	            server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids
218	        )
219	
220	    # Default grammar backends
221	    if name == "outlines":
222	        from sglang.srt.constrained.outlines_backend import OutlinesGrammarBackend
223	
224	        grammar_backend = OutlinesGrammarBackend(
225	            tokenizer,
226	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
227	        )
228	    elif name == "xgrammar":
229	        from sglang.srt.constrained.xgrammar_backend import (
230	            TokenizerNotSupportedError,
231	            XGrammarGrammarBackend,
232	        )
233	
234	        # Convert Set[int] to List[int] if needed
235	        eos_list = list(eos_token_ids) if eos_token_ids else None
236	
237	        try:
238	            grammar_backend = XGrammarGrammarBackend(
239	                tokenizer,
240	                vocab_size=vocab_size,
241	                model_eos_token_ids=eos_list,
242	                any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
243	            )
244	        except TokenizerNotSupportedError as e:
245	            logger.warning(
246	                f"Grammar backend disabled because tokenizer is not supported by XGrammar: {e}. "
247	                "Falling back to grammar_backend='none'. "
248	                "Structured outputs (JSON schema, regex, EBNF) will not be available."
249	            )
250	            server_args.grammar_backend = "none"
251	            return None
252	    elif name == "llguidance":
253	        from sglang.srt.constrained.llguidance_backend import GuidanceBackend
254	
255	        grammar_backend = GuidanceBackend(
256	            tokenizer=tokenizer,
257	            any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
258	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
259	        )
260	    elif name == "none":
261	        return None
262	    else:
263	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid grammar backend: {name}")
264	
265	    if server_args.reasoning_parser and hasattr(tokenizer, "think_end_id"):
266	        from sglang.srt.constrained.reasoner_grammar_backend import (
267	            ReasonerGrammarBackend,
268	        )
269	
270	        grammar_backend = ReasonerGrammarBackend(
271	            grammar_backend, tokenizer.think_end_id
272	        )
273	
274	    return grammar_backend

14	"""Sampling parameters for text generation."""

16	import logging
17	from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Union
18	
19	# sre_parse is deprecated in Python 3.11+, use re._parser instead
20	try:
21	    import re._parser as sre_parse
22	except ImportError:
23	    import sre_parse  # Python < 3.11

25	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-6
26	TOP_K_ALL = 1 << 30
27	
28	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

31	class SamplingParams:
32	    """
33	    The sampling parameters.
34	
35	    See docs/backend/sampling_params.md or
36	    https://docs.sglang.io/backend/sampling_params.html
37	    for the documentation.
38	    """
39	
40	    def __init__(
41	        self,
42	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
43	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
44	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
45	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
46	        temperature: float = 1.0,
47	        top_p: float = 1.0,
48	        top_k: int = -1,
49	        min_p: float = 0.0,
50	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
51	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
52	        repetition_penalty: float = 1.0,
53	        min_new_tokens: int = 0,
54	        n: int = 1,
55	        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
56	        regex: Optional[str] = None,
57	        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
58	        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
59	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
60	        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
61	        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
62	        no_stop_trim: bool = False,
63	        custom_params: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
64	        stream_interval: Optional[int] = None,
65	        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None,
66	        sampling_seed: Optional[int] = None,
67	    ) -> None:
68	        self.max_new_tokens = max_new_tokens
69	        self.stop_strs = stop
70	        if stop_token_ids:
71	            self.stop_token_ids = set(stop_token_ids)
72	        else:
73	            self.stop_token_ids = None
74	        self.stop_regex_strs = stop_regex
75	        self.temperature = temperature
76	        self.top_p = top_p
77	        self.top_k = top_k
78	        self.min_p = min_p
79	        self.frequency_penalty = frequency_penalty
80	        self.presence_penalty = presence_penalty
81	        self.repetition_penalty = repetition_penalty
82	        self.min_new_tokens = min_new_tokens
83	        self.regex = regex
84	        self.n = n
85	        self.json_schema = json_schema
86	        self.ebnf = ebnf
87	        self.structural_tag = structural_tag
88	        self.ignore_eos = ignore_eos
89	        self.skip_special_tokens = skip_special_tokens
90	        self.spaces_between_special_tokens = spaces_between_special_tokens
91	        self.no_stop_trim = no_stop_trim
92	        self.custom_params = custom_params
93	        self.stream_interval = stream_interval
94	        self.logit_bias = logit_bias
95	        self.sampling_seed = sampling_seed
96	
97	        # Process some special cases
98	        if 0 <= self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
99	            # top_k = 1 means greedy sampling
100	            self.temperature = 1.0
101	            self.top_k = 1
102	        if self.top_k == -1:
103	            self.top_k = TOP_K_ALL  # whole vocabulary
104

14	"""Constrained decoding with xgrammar backend."""
15	
16	import dataclasses
17	import json
18	import logging
19	from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Tuple, Union
20	
21	import torch
22	from xgrammar import (
23	    CompiledGrammar,
24	    GrammarCompiler,
25	    GrammarMatcher,
26	    StructuralTagItem,
27	    TokenizerInfo,
28	    allocate_token_bitmask,
29	)
