31	from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import (
32	    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
33	    BaseGrammarBackend,
34	    BaseGrammarObject,
35	    GrammarStats,
36	)
37	from sglang.srt.constrained.utils import is_legacy_structural_tag
38	from sglang.srt.utils import is_hip
39	
40	_is_hip = is_hip()
41	if _is_hip:
42	    from sgl_kernel import apply_token_bitmask_inplace_cuda
43	else:
44	    from sglang.srt.constrained.triton_ops.bitmask_ops import (
45	        apply_token_bitmask_inplace_triton,
46	    )
47	
48	
49	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
50	MAX_ROLLBACK_TOKENS = 200
51	
52	
53	class XGrammarGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
54	
55	    def __init__(
56	        self,
57	        matcher: GrammarMatcher,
58	        vocab_size: int,
59	        ctx: CompiledGrammar,
60	        override_stop_tokens: Optional[Union[List[int], int]],
61	        key_string: Optional[str] = None,  # TODO (sk): for debugging, remove later
62	        grammar_stats: Optional[GrammarStats] = GrammarStats(),
63	    ) -> None:
64	        super().__init__()
65	        self.matcher = matcher
66	        self.vocab_size = vocab_size
67	        self.ctx = ctx
68	        self.override_stop_tokens = override_stop_tokens
69	        self.accepted_tokens = []
70	        self.key_string = key_string
71	        self.grammar_stats = grammar_stats
72	
73	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
74	        if not self.is_terminated():
75	            self.current_token = token
76	            accepted = self.matcher.accept_token(token)
77	            if not accepted:
78	                # log for debugging
79	                raise ValueError(
80	                    f"Tokens not accepted: {token}\n"
81	                    f"Accepted tokens: {self.accepted_tokens}\n"
82	                    f"Key string: {self.key_string}"
83	                )
84	            else:
85	                self.accepted_tokens.append(token)
86	
87	    def rollback(self, k: int):
88	        self.matcher.rollback(k)
89	        self.accepted_tokens = self.accepted_tokens[:-k]
90	
91	    def is_terminated(self):
92	        return self.matcher.is_terminated()
93	
94	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
95	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
96	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
97	        return allocate_token_bitmask(batch_size, vocab_size)
98	
99	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
100	        self.matcher.fill_next_token_bitmask(vocab_mask, idx)
101	
102	    @staticmethod
103	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
104	        return vocab_mask.to(device, non_blocking=True)
105	
106	    def apply_vocab_mask(self, logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
107	        if (
108	            logits.device.type == "cuda"
109	            or logits.device.type == "npu"
110	            or logits.device.type == "xpu"
111	        ):
112	            if _is_hip:
113	                apply_token_bitmask_inplace_cuda(logits, vocab_mask)
114	            else:
115	                apply_token_bitmask_inplace_triton(logits, vocab_mask)
116	        elif logits.device.type == "cpu" and self.apply_vocab_mask_cpu:
117	            self.apply_vocab_mask_cpu(logits, vocab_mask)
118	        else:
119	            raise RuntimeError(f"Unsupported device: {logits.device.type}")
120	
121	    def copy(self):
122	        matcher = GrammarMatcher(
123	            self.ctx,
124	            max_rollback_tokens=MAX_ROLLBACK_TOKENS,
125	            override_stop_tokens=self.override_stop_tokens,
126	        )
127	        return XGrammarGrammar(
128	            matcher,
129	            self.vocab_size,
130	            self.ctx,
131	            self.override_stop_tokens,
132	            self.key_string,
133	            dataclasses.replace(
134	                self.grammar_stats, is_cache_hit=True, tree_traversal_time=[]
135	            ),
136	        )
137	
138	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]:
139	        s = self.matcher.find_jump_forward_string()
140	        if s:
141	            return [], s
142	        return None
143	
144	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
145	        _, data = helper
146	        return data, -1
147	
148	    def jump_and_retokenize(
149	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
150	    ):
151	        k = 0
152	        for i, old_id in enumerate(old_output_ids):
153	            if old_id == new_output_ids[i]:
154	                k = i + 1
155	            else:
156	                break
157	
158	        # rollback to the last token that is the same
159	        if k < len(old_output_ids):
160	            self.matcher.rollback(len(old_output_ids) - k)
161	
162	        for i in range(k, len(new_output_ids)):
163	            assert self.matcher.accept_token(new_output_ids[i])
164	
165	    def __repr__(self):
166	        return f"XGrammarGrammar({self.key_string=}, {self.accepted_tokens=}, {self.current_token=})"
167	
168	
169	class TokenizerNotSupportedError(Exception):
170	    """Raised when tokenizer is not supported by XGrammar backend."""
171	
172	    pass
173	
174	
175	class XGrammarGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
176	    def __init__(
177	        self,
178	        tokenizer,
179	        vocab_size: int,
180	        model_eos_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
181	        any_whitespace: bool = True,
182	    ):
183	        super().__init__()
184	
185	        if hasattr(tokenizer, "init_xgrammar"):
186	            # For special tokenizer
187	            tokenizer_info, override_stop_tokens = tokenizer.init_xgrammar()
188	
189	            if tokenizer_info is None:
190	                # Not supported tokenizer
191	                raise TokenizerNotSupportedError(
192	                    f"Tokenizer type {type(tokenizer).__name__} is not supported by XGrammar"
193	                )
194	        else:
195	            # Create TokenizerInfo with model's EOS tokens as the authoritative stop tokens
196	            # This ensures consistency between what the model considers EOS and what XGrammar uses
197	            try:
198	                tokenizer_info = TokenizerInfo.from_huggingface(
199	                    tokenizer, vocab_size=vocab_size, stop_token_ids=model_eos_token_ids
200	                )
201	                override_stop_tokens = None
202	            except Exception as e:
203	                raise TokenizerNotSupportedError(
204	                    f"Failed to create XGrammar TokenizerInfo from tokenizer: {e}"
205	                )
206	
207	        self.grammar_compiler = GrammarCompiler(tokenizer_info=tokenizer_info)
208	        self.vocab_size = vocab_size
209	        self.override_stop_tokens = override_stop_tokens
210	        self.any_whitespace = any_whitespace
211	
212	    @staticmethod
213	    def _sanitize_structural_format(structural_format):
214	        """Recursively replace missing json_schema fields with an empty schema."""
215	        if not isinstance(structural_format, dict):
216	            return
217	
218	        fmt_type = structural_format.get("type")
219	        if fmt_type in {"json_schema", "qwen_xml_parameter"}:
220	            if structural_format.get("json_schema") is None:
221	                structural_format["json_schema"] = {}
222	
223	        if fmt_type == "tag":
224	            XGrammarGrammarBackend._sanitize_structural_format(
225	                structural_format.get("content")
226	            )
227	        elif fmt_type in {"sequence", "or"}:
228	            for element in structural_format.get("elements", []):
229	                XGrammarGrammarBackend._sanitize_structural_format(element)
230	        elif fmt_type in {"triggered_tags", "tags_with_separator"}:
231	            for tag in structural_format.get("tags", []):
232	                XGrammarGrammarBackend._sanitize_structural_format(tag)
233	
234	    @staticmethod
235	    def _sanitize_structural_tag_structures(structural_tag: Dict) -> None:
236	        for structure in structural_tag.get("structures", []):
237	            if structure.get("schema") is None:
238	                structure["schema"] = {}
239	
240	    def _from_context(
241	        self, ctx: CompiledGrammar, key_string: str, grammar_stats: GrammarStats
242	    ) -> XGrammarGrammar:
243	        matcher = GrammarMatcher(
244	            ctx,
245	            max_rollback_tokens=MAX_ROLLBACK_TOKENS,
246	            override_stop_tokens=self.override_stop_tokens,
247	        )
248	        return XGrammarGrammar(
249	            matcher,
250	            self.vocab_size,
251	            ctx,
252	            self.override_stop_tokens,
253	            key_string,
254	            grammar_stats,
255	        )
256	
257	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
258	        try:
259	            if key_string == "$$ANY$$":
260	                # Note: This builtin JSON grammar includes *all* valid JSON (including, for example, arrays at the root)
261	                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_builtin_json_grammar()
262	            else:
263	                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_json_schema(
264	                    schema=key_string, any_whitespace=self.any_whitespace
265	                )
266	
267	        except (RuntimeError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError, UnicodeDecodeError) as e:
268	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
269	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
270	        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="json"))
271	
272	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
273	        try:
274	            ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_grammar(key_string)
275	        except RuntimeError as e:
276	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid ebnf: {key_string=}, {e=}")
277	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
278	        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="ebnf"))
279	
280	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
281	        try:
282	            ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_regex(key_string)
283	        except RuntimeError as e:
284	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid regex: {key_string=}, {e=}")
285	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
286	        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="regex"))
287	
288	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
289	        try:
290	            # TODO(dark): it's REALLY stupid to construct object from string and decode it again
291	            structural_tag = json.loads(key_string)
292	            if is_legacy_structural_tag(structural_tag):
293	                self._sanitize_structural_tag_structures(structural_tag)
294	                tags = [
295	                    StructuralTagItem(
296	                        begin=structure["begin"],
297	                        schema=json.dumps(structure["schema"]),
298	                        end=structure["end"],
299	                    )
300	                    for structure in structural_tag["structures"]
301	                ]
302	                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_structural_tag(
303	                    tags, structural_tag["triggers"]
304	                )
305	            else:
306	                format_dict = structural_tag.get("format")
307	                if isinstance(format_dict, dict):
308	                    self._sanitize_structural_format(format_dict)
309	                    structural_tag["format"] = format_dict
310	                    key_string = json.dumps(structural_tag)
311	                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_structural_tag(key_string)
312	        except (RuntimeError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError) as e:
313	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid structural_tag: {key_string=}, {e=}")
314	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
315	        return self._from_context(
316	            ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="structural_tag")
317	        )
318	
319	    def reset(self):
320	        self.grammar_compiler.clear_cache()
321	
322	
323	def demo_test():
324	    from transformers import AutoConfig, AutoTokenizer
325	
326	    from sglang.test.test_utils import DEFAULT_MODEL_NAME_FOR_TEST
327	
328	    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(DEFAULT_MODEL_NAME_FOR_TEST)
329	    hf_config = AutoConfig.from_pretrained(DEFAULT_MODEL_NAME_FOR_TEST)
330	
331	    # Should use vocab size from model config
332	    vocab_size = hf_config.vocab_size
333	    eos_token_id = tokenizer.eos_token_id
334	
335	    backend = XGrammarGrammarBackend(
336	        tokenizer, vocab_size=vocab_size, model_eos_token_ids=[eos_token_id]
337	    )
338	    regex = r"hello (world|there)"
339	    grammar = backend.dispatch_regex(regex)
340	    tokens = [
341	        tokenizer.encode(t, add_special_tokens=False)[0] for t in ["hello", " world"]
342	    ]
343	
344	    # Test termination
345	    grammar.accept_token(tokens[0])  # accept "hello"
346	    grammar.accept_token(tokens[1])  # accept " world"
347	    grammar.accept_token(eos_token_id)  # accept EOS
348	    assert grammar.is_terminated()
349	
350	    # Test rollback the terminated state
351	    grammar.rollback(1)
352	    assert not grammar.is_terminated()
353	
354	
355	if __name__ == "__main__":
356	    demo_test()
357

14	"""Constrained decoding with outlines backend."""
15	
16	import json
17	import logging
18	from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Tuple, Union
19	
20	import interegular
21	import torch
22	from outlines.fsm.guide import RegexGuide
23	from outlines.models.transformers import TransformerTokenizer
24	from pydantic import BaseModel
25	
26	from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import (
27	    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
28	    BaseGrammarBackend,
29	    BaseGrammarObject,
30	)
31	from sglang.srt.constrained.outlines_jump_forward import OutlinesJumpForwardMap
32	
33	try:
34	    from outlines.fsm.json_schema import build_regex_from_schema
35	except ImportError:
36	    from outlines_core.fsm.json_schema import build_regex_from_schema
37	
38	
39	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
40	
41	
42	class OutlinesGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
43	    def __init__(
44	        self,
45	        guide: RegexGuide,
46	        jump_forward_map: Union[OutlinesJumpForwardMap, None],
47	    ) -> None:
48	        super().__init__()
49	        self.guide = guide
50	        self.jump_forward_map = jump_forward_map
51	        self.state = 0
52	
53	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
54	        self.state = self.guide.get_next_state(self.state, token)
55	
56	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
57	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
58	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
59	        return torch.zeros(batch_size, vocab_size, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
60	
61	    @staticmethod
62	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
63	        return vocab_mask
64	
65	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
66	        tokens = torch.tensor(
67	            self.guide.get_next_instruction(self.state).tokens, dtype=torch.int64
68	        ).to(vocab_mask.device, non_blocking=True)
69	        vocab_mask = vocab_mask[idx]
70	        vocab_mask.fill_(1)
71	        vocab_mask.scatter_(0, tokens, torch.zeros_like(tokens, dtype=torch.bool))
72	
73	    @staticmethod
74	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor):
75	        logits.masked_fill_(vocab_mask, float("-inf"))
76	
77	    def copy(self):
78	        return OutlinesGrammar(self.guide, self.jump_forward_map)
79	
80	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple]:
81	        if not self.jump_forward_map:
82	            return None
83	
84	        jump_forward_bytes = self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_byte(self.state)
85	        if jump_forward_bytes is None or len(jump_forward_bytes) <= 1:
86	            return None
87	
88	        # preprocess the jump forward string
89	        suffix_bytes = []
90	        continuation_range = range(0x80, 0xC0)
91	        cur_state = self.state
92	        while (
93	            len(jump_forward_bytes) and jump_forward_bytes[0][0] in continuation_range
94	        ):
95	            # continuation bytes
96	            byte_edge = jump_forward_bytes.pop(0)
97	            suffix_bytes.append(byte_edge[0])
98	            cur_state = byte_edge[1]
99	
100	        suffix_tokens = [f"<0x{hex(b)[2:].upper()}>" for b in suffix_bytes]
101	        suffix_ids = tokenizer.convert_tokens_to_ids(suffix_tokens)
102	        return suffix_ids, cur_state
103	
104	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
105	        _, cur_state = helper
106	        return self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_symbol(cur_state)
107	
108	    def jump_and_retokenize(
109	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
110	    ):
111	        self.state = next_state
112	
113	
114	class OutlinesGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
115	    def __init__(
116	        self,
117	        tokenizer,
118	        whitespace_pattern: str | None,
119	    ):
120	        super().__init__()
121	
122	        try:
123	            self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)
124	        except AttributeError:
125	            # FIXME: tmp fix for chatglm2 & chatglm3 (pad_token_id=0)
126	            origin_pad_token_id = tokenizer.pad_token_id
127	
128	            def fset(self, value):
129	                self._value = value
130	
131	            type(tokenizer).pad_token_id = property(
132	                fget=type(tokenizer).pad_token_id.fget, fset=fset
133	            )
134	            self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)
135	            self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.pad_token_id = origin_pad_token_id
136	            self.outlines_tokenizer.pad_token_id = origin_pad_token_id
137	            self.outlines_tokenizer.pad_token = (
138	                self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.pad_token
139	            )
140	            self.outlines_tokenizer.vocabulary = (
141	                self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.get_vocab()
142	            )
143	        self.whitespace_pattern = whitespace_pattern
144	
145	    def _compile_regex(self, regex: str) -> Optional[OutlinesGrammar]:
146	        try:
147	            if hasattr(RegexGuide, "from_regex"):
148	                # outlines >= 0.1.1
149	                guide = RegexGuide.from_regex(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
150	            else:
151	                # outlines <= 0.0.46
152	                guide = RegexGuide(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
153	        except interegular.patterns.InvalidSyntax as e:
154	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid regex schema: {regex=}, {e=}")
155	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
156	
157	        jump_forward_map = None
158	        return OutlinesGrammar(guide, jump_forward_map)
159	
160	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str):
161	        return super().dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
162	
163	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str):
164	        return super().dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
165	
166	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str):
167	        try:
168	            regex = build_regex_from_object(
169	                key_string,
170	                whitespace_pattern=self.whitespace_pattern,
171	            )
172	        except (NotImplementedError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError, ValueError) as e:
173	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
174	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
175	        return self._compile_regex(regex)
176	
177	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str):
178	        return self._compile_regex(key_string)
179	
180	
181	def build_regex_from_object(
182	    object: Union[str, BaseModel, Dict], whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None
183	):
184	    if isinstance(object, type(BaseModel)):
185	        schema = json.dumps(object.model_json_schema())
186	    elif isinstance(object, Dict):
187	        schema = json.dumps(object)
188	    else:
189	        schema = object
190	    return build_regex_from_schema(schema, whitespace_pattern)
191

Perfect! Now I have enough information to provide a comprehensive report. Let me compile the findings:

## SGLang Programming API - Comprehensive Exploration Report

Based on my thorough exploration of the SGLang codebase, here's a detailed analysis of how users write SGLang programs and how constraint parameters flow through the system:

### 1. **`gen()` Function Signature**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/lang/api.py:75-139`

The `gen()` function accepts constraints directly as keyword arguments:

```python
def gen(
    name: Optional[str] = None,
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    min_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    n: Optional[int] = None,
    stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
    temperature: Optional[float] = None,
    top_p: Optional[float] = None,
    top_k: Optional[int] = None,
    min_p: Optional[float] = None,
    frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
    presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
    ignore_eos: Optional[bool] = None,
    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
    dtype: Optional[Union[type, str]] = None,
    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    choices_method: Optional[ChoicesSamplingMethod] = None,
    regex: Optional[str] = None,
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
):
```

**Key constraint parameters:**
- `regex`: A regular expression pattern string for constrained decoding
- `json_schema`: A JSON schema string (as JSON string representation)
- `dtype`: Type constraint (int, float, str, bool) - converted to regex internally
- `choices`: For discrete choice selection (uses `SglSelect` instead of `SglGen`)

### 2. **User-Facing Language Constructs**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/lang/api.py`

Users write SGLang programs using decorators and primitives:

- **`@sgl.function` decorator**: Line 23-32 - decorates functions that take a state object `s` as first argument
- **Language primitives:**
  - `sgl.gen()` - generation with constraints
  - `sgl.gen_int()` - generation of integers (dtype=int)
  - `sgl.gen_string()` - generation of strings (dtype=str)
  - `sgl.select()` - discrete choice selection
  - `sgl.system()`, `sgl.user()`, `sgl.assistant()` - role markers for chat
  - `sgl.image()`, `sgl.video()` - multimodal inputs

**Practical usage example from test file** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/test/test_programs.py:110-127`):

```python
@sgl.function
def decode_json(s):
    s += "Generate a JSON object...\n"
    with s.var_scope("json_output"):
        s += "{\n"
        s += '  "name": ' + sgl.gen(regex=REGEX_STR) + ",\n"
        s += '  "population": ' + sgl.gen(regex=REGEX_INT, stop=[" ", "\n"]) + ",\n"
        s += "}"
```

### 3. **Intermediate Representation (IR) Layer**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/lang/ir.py`

The `gen()` function creates an `SglGen` IR object that encapsulates constraints:

```python
class SglGen(SglExpr):
    def __init__(
        self,
        name: Optional[str] = None,
        max_new_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
        min_new_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
        n: Optional[int] = None,
        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
        temperature: Optional[float] = None,
        top_p: Optional[float] = None,
        top_k: Optional[int] = None,
        min_p: Optional[float] = None,
        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        ignore_eos: Optional[bool] = None,
        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
        dtype: Optional[type] = None,
        regex: Optional[str] = None,
        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
    ):
        super().__init__()
        self.sampling_params = SglSamplingParams(
            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
            min_new_tokens=min_new_tokens,
            n=n,
            stop=stop,
            stop_regex=stop_regex,
            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
            temperature=temperature,
            top_p=top_p,
            top_k=top_k,
            min_p=min_p,
            frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
            presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
            return_logprob=return_logprob,
            logprob_start_len=logprob_start_len,
            top_logprobs_num=top_logprobs_num,
            return_text_in_logprobs=return_text_in_logprobs,
            dtype=dtype,
            regex=regex,
            json_schema=json_schema,
        )
```

**SglSamplingParams dataclass** (lines 17-62):
- Stores all sampling and constraint parameters
- Has methods to convert to different backend formats (`to_openai_kwargs()`, `to_srt_kwargs()`, etc.)

### 4. **Constraint Flow Through Execution**

**StreamExecutor class** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/lang/interpreter.py:250-823`)

The execution flow:

1. **Expression submission** (line 318-324): `StreamExecutor.submit()` queues expressions
2. **Execution routing** (line 437-479): `_execute()` dispatches based on expression type
3. **Generation execution** (line 569-599): `_execute_gen()` handles `SglGen`
4. **Parameter resolution** (line 775-822): `_resolve_sampling_params()` merges defaults with overrides:

```python
def _resolve_sampling_params(self, sampling_params):
    """
    Construct sampling param based on default + override values
    """
    clone = copy.deepcopy(self.default_sampling_para)
    
    for item in [
        "max_new_tokens", "min_new_tokens", "n", "stop", "stop_token_ids",
        "stop_regex", "temperature", "top_p", "top_k", "min_p",
        "frequency_penalty", "presence_penalty", "ignore_eos",
        "return_logprob", "logprob_start_len", "top_logprobs_num",
        "return_text_in_logprobs", "dtype", "regex", "json_schema"  # KEY
    ]:
        value = getattr(sampling_params, item, None)
        if value is not None:
            setattr(clone, item, value)
    
    return clone
```

### 5. **Backend Constraint Handling**

**RuntimeEndpoint backend** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/lang/backend/runtime_endpoint.py:127-196`)

Constraint conversion happens in `_handle_dtype_to_regex()` (lines 127-157):

```python
def _handle_dtype_to_regex(self, sampling_params: SglSamplingParams):
    if sampling_params.dtype is None:
        return
    
    dtype_regex = None
    if sampling_params.dtype in ["int", int]:
        dtype_regex = REGEX_INT  # r"[-+]?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
    elif sampling_params.dtype in ["float", float]:
        dtype_regex = REGEX_FLOAT  # r"[-+]?[0-9]*\.?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
    elif sampling_params.dtype in ["str", str]:
        dtype_regex = REGEX_STR  # r"\"[\w\d\s]*\""
    elif sampling_params.dtype in ["bool", bool]:
        dtype_regex = REGEX_BOOL  # r"(True|False)"
    
    # dtype takes precedence over regex
    if dtype_regex is not None and sampling_params.regex is not None:
        warnings.warn("Both dtype and regex are set. Only dtype will be used.")
    
    sampling_params.regex = dtype_regex
```

The `generate()` method (lines 159-196) converts to SRT kwargs:

```python
data = {
    "text": s.text_,
    "sampling_params": {
        "skip_special_tokens": global_config.skip_special_tokens_in_output,
        "spaces_between_special_tokens": global_config.spaces_between_special_tokens_in_out,
        **sampling_params.to_srt_kwargs(),  # Includes regex and json_schema
    },
}
```

### 6. **Constraint Test Examples**

**JSON Schema constraints** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/test/kits/json_constrained_kit.py:8-19`):

```python
json_schema = json.dumps({
```

Perfect! Now I have enough information to provide a comprehensive report. Let me compile the findings:

## SGLang Programming API - Comprehensive Exploration Report

Based on my thorough exploration of the SGLang codebase, here's a detailed analysis of how users write SGLang programs and how constraint parameters flow through the system:

### 1. **`gen()` Function Signature**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/lang/api.py:75-139`

The `gen()` function accepts constraints directly as keyword arguments:

```python
def gen(
    name: Optional[str] = None,
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    min_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    n: Optional[int] = None,
    stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
    temperature: Optional[float] = None,
    top_p: Optional[float] = None,
    top_k: Optional[int] = None,
    min_p: Optional[float] = None,
    frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
    presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
    ignore_eos: Optional[bool] = None,
    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
    dtype: Optional[Union[type, str]] = None,
    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    choices_method: Optional[ChoicesSamplingMethod] = None,
    regex: Optional[str] = None,
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
):
```

**Key constraint parameters:**
- `regex`: A regular expression pattern string for constrained decoding
- `json_schema`: A JSON schema string (as JSON string representation)
- `dtype`: Type constraint (int, float, str, bool) - converted to regex internally
- `choices`: For discrete choice selection (uses `SglSelect` instead of `SglGen`)

### 2. **User-Facing Language Constructs**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/lang/api.py`

Users write SGLang programs using decorators and primitives:

- **`@sgl.function` decorator**: Line 23-32 - decorates functions that take a state object `s` as first argument
- **Language primitives:**
  - `sgl.gen()` - generation with constraints
  - `sgl.gen_int()` - generation of integers (dtype=int)
  - `sgl.gen_string()` - generation of strings (dtype=str)
  - `sgl.select()` - discrete choice selection
  - `sgl.system()`, `sgl.user()`, `sgl.assistant()` - role markers for chat
  - `sgl.image()`, `sgl.video()` - multimodal inputs

**Practical usage example from test file** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/test/test_programs.py:110-127`):

```python
@sgl.function
def decode_json(s):
    s += "Generate a JSON object...\n"
    with s.var_scope("json_output"):
        s += "{\n"
        s += '  "name": ' + sgl.gen(regex=REGEX_STR) + ",\n"
        s += '  "population": ' + sgl.gen(regex=REGEX_INT, stop=[" ", "\n"]) + ",\n"
        s += "}"
```

### 3. **Intermediate Representation (IR) Layer**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/lang/ir.py`

The `gen()` function creates an `SglGen` IR object that encapsulates constraints:

```python
class SglGen(SglExpr):
    def __init__(
        self,
        name: Optional[str] = None,
        max_new_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
        min_new_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
        n: Optional[int] = None,
        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
        temperature: Optional[float] = None,
        top_p: Optional[float] = None,
        top_k: Optional[int] = None,
        min_p: Optional[float] = None,
        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        ignore_eos: Optional[bool] = None,
        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
        dtype: Optional[type] = None,
        regex: Optional[str] = None,
        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
    ):
        super().__init__()
        self.sampling_params = SglSamplingParams(
            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
            min_new_tokens=min_new_tokens,
            n=n,
            stop=stop,
            stop_regex=stop_regex,
            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
            temperature=temperature,
            top_p=top_p,
            top_k=top_k,
            min_p=min_p,
            frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
            presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
            return_logprob=return_logprob,
            logprob_start_len=logprob_start_len,
            top_logprobs_num=top_logprobs_num,
            return_text_in_logprobs=return_text_in_logprobs,
            dtype=dtype,
            regex=regex,
            json_schema=json_schema,
        )
```

**SglSamplingParams dataclass** (lines 17-62):
- Stores all sampling and constraint parameters
- Has methods to convert to different backend formats (`to_openai_kwargs()`, `to_srt_kwargs()`, etc.)

### 4. **Constraint Flow Through Execution**

**StreamExecutor class** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/lang/interpreter.py:250-823`)

The execution flow:

1. **Expression submission** (line 318-324): `StreamExecutor.submit()` queues expressions
2. **Execution routing** (line 437-479): `_execute()` dispatches based on expression type
3. **Generation execution** (line 569-599): `_execute_gen()` handles `SglGen`
4. **Parameter resolution** (line 775-822): `_resolve_sampling_params()` merges defaults with overrides:

```python
def _resolve_sampling_params(self, sampling_params):
    """
    Construct sampling param based on default + override values
    """
    clone = copy.deepcopy(self.default_sampling_para)
    
    for item in [
        "max_new_tokens", "min_new_tokens", "n", "stop", "stop_token_ids",
        "stop_regex", "temperature", "top_p", "top_k", "min_p",
        "frequency_penalty", "presence_penalty", "ignore_eos",
        "return_logprob", "logprob_start_len", "top_logprobs_num",
        "return_text_in_logprobs", "dtype", "regex", "json_schema"  # KEY
    ]:
        value = getattr(sampling_params, item, None)
        if value is not None:
            setattr(clone, item, value)
    
    return clone
```

### 5. **Backend Constraint Handling**

**RuntimeEndpoint backend** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/lang/backend/runtime_endpoint.py:127-196`)

Constraint conversion happens in `_handle_dtype_to_regex()` (lines 127-157):

```python
def _handle_dtype_to_regex(self, sampling_params: SglSamplingParams):
    if sampling_params.dtype is None:
        return
    
    dtype_regex = None
    if sampling_params.dtype in ["int", int]:
        dtype_regex = REGEX_INT  # r"[-+]?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
    elif sampling_params.dtype in ["float", float]:
        dtype_regex = REGEX_FLOAT  # r"[-+]?[0-9]*\.?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
    elif sampling_params.dtype in ["str", str]:
        dtype_regex = REGEX_STR  # r"\"[\w\d\s]*\""
    elif sampling_params.dtype in ["bool", bool]:
        dtype_regex = REGEX_BOOL  # r"(True|False)"
    
    # dtype takes precedence over regex
    if dtype_regex is not None and sampling_params.regex is not None:
        warnings.warn("Both dtype and regex are set. Only dtype will be used.")
    
    sampling_params.regex = dtype_regex
```

The `generate()` method (lines 159-196) converts to SRT kwargs:

```python
data = {
    "text": s.text_,
    "sampling_params": {
        "skip_special_tokens": global_config.skip_special_tokens_in_output,
        "spaces_between_special_tokens": global_config.spaces_between_special_tokens_in_out,
        **sampling_params.to_srt_kwargs(),  # Includes regex and json_schema
    },
}
```

```python
        },

```

**Regex constraints** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493100829-mcjmq3/python/sglang/test/kits/regex_constrained_kit.py:6-70`):

1	"""The intermediate representation."""
2	
3	import dataclasses
4	import inspect
5	import warnings
6	from typing import List, Optional, Union
7	
8	from sglang.global_config import global_config
9	from sglang.lang.choices import ChoicesSamplingMethod
10	
11	REGEX_INT = r"[-+]?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
12	REGEX_FLOAT = r"[-+]?[0-9]*\.?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
13	REGEX_BOOL = r"(True|False)"
14	REGEX_STR = r"\"[\w\d\s]*\""  # bugs with regex r"\".*\"" in interegular pkg
15	
16	
17	@dataclasses.dataclass
18	class SglSamplingParams:
19	    max_new_tokens: int = 128
20	    min_new_tokens: int = 0
21	    n: int = 1
22	    stop: Union[str, List[str]] = ()
23	    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = ()
24	    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = ()
25	    temperature: float = 1.0
26	    top_p: float = 1.0
27	    top_k: int = -1  # -1 means disable
28	    min_p: float = 0.0
29	    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
30	    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
31	    ignore_eos: bool = False
32	    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None
33	    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = (None,)
34	    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = (None,)
35	    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = (None,)
36	    json_schema: Optional[str] = None
37	
38	    # for constrained generation, not included in to_xxx_kwargs
39	    dtype: Optional[str] = None
40	    regex: Optional[str] = None
41	
42	    def clone(self):
43	        return SglSamplingParams(
44	            self.max_new_tokens,
45	            self.min_new_tokens,
46	            self.n,
47	            self.stop,
48	            self.stop_token_ids,
49	            self.stop_regex,
50	            self.temperature,
51	            self.top_p,
52	            self.top_k,
53	            self.min_p,
54	            self.frequency_penalty,
55	            self.presence_penalty,
56	            self.ignore_eos,
57	            self.return_logprob,
58	            self.logprob_start_len,
59	            self.top_logprobs_num,
60	            self.return_text_in_logprobs,
61	            self.json_schema,
62	        )
63	
64	    def to_openai_kwargs(self):
65	        # OpenAI does not support top_k, so we drop it here
66	        if self.regex is not None:
67	            warnings.warn("Regular expression is not supported in the OpenAI backend.")
68	        return {
69	            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
70	            "max_completion_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
71	            "n": self.n,
72	            "stop": self.stop or None,
73	            "temperature": self.temperature,
74	            "top_p": self.top_p,
75	            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
76	            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
77	        }
78	
79	    def to_vertexai_kwargs(self):
80	        if self.regex is not None:
81	            warnings.warn(
82	                "Regular expression is not supported in the VertexAI backend."
83	            )
84	        return {
85	            "candidate_count": 1,
86	            "max_output_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
87	            "stop_sequences": self.stop,
88	            "temperature": self.temperature,
89	            "top_p": self.top_p,
90	            "top_k": self.top_k if self.top_k > 0 else None,
91	        }
92	
93	    def to_anthropic_kwargs(self):
94	        # Anthropic does not support frequency_penalty or presence_penalty, so we drop it here
95	        if self.regex is not None:
96	            warnings.warn(
97	                "Regular expression is not supported in the Anthropic backend."
98	            )
99	        return {
100	            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
101	            "stop_sequences": (
102	                self.stop if isinstance(self.stop, (list, tuple)) else [self.stop]
103	            ),
104	            "temperature": self.temperature,
105	            "top_p": self.top_p,
106	            "top_k": self.top_k,
107	        }
108	
109	    def to_litellm_kwargs(self):
110	        if self.regex is not None:
111	            warnings.warn("Regular expression is not supported in the LiteLLM backend.")
112	        return {
113	            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
114	            "stop": self.stop or None,
115	            "temperature": self.temperature,
116	            "top_p": self.top_p,
117	            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
118	            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
119	        }
120	
121	    def to_srt_kwargs(self):
122	        return {
123	            "max_new_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
124	            "min_new_tokens": self.min_new_tokens,
125	            "n": self.n,

61	def _extract_max_dynamic_patch(request: ChatCompletionRequest):
62	    img_vals = []
63	    vid_vals = []
64	    for msg in request.messages or []:
65	        content = getattr(msg, "content", None)
66	        if not isinstance(content, list):
67	            continue
68	        for part in content:
69	            # pydantic object or dict type
70	            if getattr(part, "type", None) == "image_url":
71	                iu = getattr(part, "image_url", None)
72	                mdp = getattr(iu, "max_dynamic_patch", None) if iu else None
73	                if mdp is not None:
74	                    img_vals.append(int(mdp))
75	            elif getattr(part, "type", None) == "video_url":
76	                vu = getattr(part, "video_url", None)
77	                mdp = getattr(vu, "max_dynamic_patch", None) if vu else None
78	                if mdp is not None:
79	                    vid_vals.append(int(mdp))
80	
81	    # TODO(yuan-luo): per-item max_dynamic_patch for both image and video
82	    img_max_dynamic_patch = min(img_vals) if img_vals else None
83	    vid_max_dynamic_patch = min(vid_vals) if vid_vals else None
84	    return img_max_dynamic_patch, vid_max_dynamic_patch

40	class Sampler(nn.Module):
41	    def __init__(self):
42	        super().__init__()
43	        self.use_nan_detection = get_global_server_args().enable_nan_detection
44	        self.tp_sync_group = get_tp_group().device_group
45	        if is_dp_attention_enabled():
46	            self.tp_sync_group = get_attention_tp_group().device_group
47	
48	        self.rl_on_policy_target = get_global_server_args().rl_on_policy_target
49	        # In RL on-policy mode, deterministic inference is automatically enabled.
50	        self.enable_deterministic = (
51	            get_global_server_args().enable_deterministic_inference
52	        )
53	        # In RL on-policy mode, we use log_softmax to compute logprobs to match the trainer.
54	        self.use_log_softmax_logprob = self.rl_on_policy_target is not None
55	        self.use_ascend_backend = get_global_server_args().sampling_backend == "ascend"
56	
57	    def _preprocess_logits(
58	        self, logits: torch.Tensor, sampling_info: SamplingBatchInfo
59	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
60	        """Apply custom logit processors and handle NaN detection."""
61	        # Apply the custom logit processors if registered in the sampling info
62	        if sampling_info.has_custom_logit_processor:
63	            apply_custom_logit_processor(logits, sampling_info)
64	
65	        # Detect and handle NaN values in logits
66	        if self.use_nan_detection and torch.any(torch.isnan(logits)):
67	            logger.warning("Detected errors during sampling! NaN in the logits.")
68	            logits = torch.where(
69	                torch.isnan(logits), torch.full_like(logits, -1e5), logits
70	            )
71	            if crash_on_warnings():
72	                raise ValueError("Detected errors during sampling! NaN in the logits.")
73	
74	        return logits
75	
76	    def forward(
77	        self,
78	        logits_output: LogitsProcessorOutput,
79	        sampling_info: SamplingBatchInfo,
80	        return_logprob: bool,
81	        top_logprobs_nums: List[int],
82	        token_ids_logprobs: List[List[int]],
83	        positions: torch.Tensor,
84	    ):
85	        """Run a sampler & compute logprobs and update logits_output accordingly.
86	
87	        Args:
88	            logits_output: The logits from the model forward
89	            sampling_info: Metadata for sampling
90	            return_logprob: If set, store the output logprob information to
91	                logits_output
92	            top_logprobs_nums: Number of top lobprobs per sequence in a batch
93	            token_ids_logprobs: Per-sequence list of specific token IDs to retrieve
94	                logprobs for. Each element is a list of token IDs (or None) for one
95	                sequence in the batch. This is used in speculative decoding.
96	            positions: The positions of the tokens in the sequence. Used for deterministic sampling
97	                to get the unique seed for each position.
98	        """
99	        logits = logits_output.next_token_logits
100	
101	        # Preprocess logits (custom processors and NaN handling)
102	        logits = self._preprocess_logits(logits, sampling_info)
103	
104	        if sampling_info.is_all_greedy:
105	            # Use torch.argmax if all requests use greedy sampling
106	            batch_next_token_ids = torch.argmax(logits, -1)
107	            if return_logprob:
108	                original_logprobs = logprobs = torch.nn.functional.log_softmax(
109	                    logits, dim=-1
110	                )
111	        else:
112	            simple_sampling_case = (
113	                not sampling_info.need_top_p_sampling
114	                and not sampling_info.need_top_k_sampling
115	                and not sampling_info.need_min_p_sampling
116	            )
117	
118	            # If requested, cache original logprobs before temperature scaling.
119	            if return_logprob and SGLANG_RETURN_ORIGINAL_LOGPROB:
120	                original_logprobs = torch.log_softmax(logits, dim=-1)
121	
122	            # In RL on-policy mode, we use log_softmax to compute logprobs to match the trainer.
123	            logprobs_via_logsoftmax_kernel = None
124	            if self.rl_on_policy_target is not None:
125	                # TODO: use more inplace ops to save memory
126	                logits_div_temperature = (
127	                    logits.bfloat16().div(sampling_info.temperatures).bfloat16()
128	                )
129	                logprobs_via_logsoftmax_kernel = torch.log_softmax(
130	                    logits_div_temperature, dim=-1
131	                )
132	                del logits_div_temperature
133	
134	            if self.use_ascend_backend:
135	                # Ascend backend: sample from logits directly.
136	                batch_next_token_ids, logprobs = self._forward_ascend_backend(
137	                    logits, sampling_info, simple_sampling_case, return_logprob
138	                )
139	            elif (
140	                self.use_log_softmax_logprob
141	                and self.enable_deterministic
142	                and simple_sampling_case
143	            ):
144	                # RL on-policy path: sample from logprobs to match the trainer.
145	                batch_next_token_ids = self._sample_from_logprobs(
146	                    logprobs_via_logsoftmax_kernel,
147	                    sampling_info,
148	                    positions,
149	                )
150	                if return_logprob and not SGLANG_RETURN_ORIGINAL_LOGPROB:
151	                    logprobs = logprobs_via_logsoftmax_kernel
152	            else:
153	                # Standard path: do softmax and sample from probs.
154	                logits.div_(sampling_info.temperatures)
155	
156	                # In-place op to save memory
157	                logits[:] = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
158	                probs = logits
159	
160	                batch_next_token_ids = self._sample_from_probs(
161	                    probs, sampling_info, positions, simple_sampling_case
162	                )
163	                if return_logprob and not SGLANG_RETURN_ORIGINAL_LOGPROB:
164	                    logprobs = (
165	                        logprobs_via_logsoftmax_kernel
166	                        if logprobs_via_logsoftmax_kernel is not None
167	                        else torch.log(probs)
168	                    )
169	                del probs
170	
171	        # Attach logprobs to logits_output (in-place modification)
172	        if return_logprob:
173	            if SGLANG_RETURN_ORIGINAL_LOGPROB:
174	                logprobs = original_logprobs
175	            self._attach_logprobs_to_output(
176	                logits_output,
177	                logprobs,
178	                top_logprobs_nums,
179	                token_ids_logprobs,
180	                sampling_info,
181	                batch_next_token_ids,
182	            )
183	
184	        self._sync_token_ids_across_tp(batch_next_token_ids, sampling_info)
185	
186	        return batch_next_token_ids
187

21	@dataclasses.dataclass

49	    acc_linear_penalties: torch.Tensor = None  # Used in the overlap mode
50	
51	    # Whether any request has custom logit processor
52	    has_custom_logit_processor: bool = False
53	    # Custom parameters
54	    custom_params: Optional[List[Optional[Dict[str, Any]]]] = None
55	    # Custom logit processor
56	    custom_logit_processor: Optional[

59

61	    sampling_seed: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None

67	    logit_bias: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None

73	
74	        reqs = batch.reqs
75	        device = batch.device
76	        temperatures = torch.tensor(
77	            [r.sampling_params.temperature for r in reqs],
78	            dtype=torch.float,
79	            device=device,
80	        ).view(-1, 1)
81	        top_ps = torch.tensor(
82	            [r.sampling_params.top_p for r in reqs], dtype=torch.float, device=device
83	        )
84	        top_ks = torch.tensor(
85	            [r.sampling_params.top_k for r in reqs], dtype=torch.int32, device=device
86	        )
87	        min_ps = torch.tensor(
88	            [r.sampling_params.min_p for r in reqs], dtype=torch.float, device=device
89	        )
90	        sampling_seed = (
91	            torch.tensor(
92	                [
93	                    (
94	                        r.sampling_params.sampling_seed
95	                        if r.sampling_params.sampling_seed is not None
96	                        else 42
97	                    )
98	                    for r in reqs
99	                ],
100	                dtype=torch.int64,
101	                device=device,
102	            )
103	            if enable_deterministic
104	            else None
105	        )
106	
107	        logit_bias = None
108	        if any(r.sampling_params.logit_bias is not None for r in reqs):
109	            logit_bias = torch.zeros(len(reqs), vocab_size, device=device)
110	            for i, r in enumerate(reqs):
111	                if r.sampling_params.logit_bias is not None:
112	                    for key, value in r.sampling_params.logit_bias.items():
113	                        logit_bias[i, int(key)] = value
114	
115	        # Check if any request has custom logit processor
116	        has_custom_logit_processor = (
117	            global_server_args.enable_custom_logit_processor
118	            and any(r.custom_logit_processor for r in reqs)  # check the flag first.
119	        )  # then check the requests.
120	
121	        if has_custom_logit_processor:
122	            # Merge the same type of custom logit processors together
123	            processor_dict = {}
124	            for i, r in enumerate(reqs):
125	                if r.custom_logit_processor is None:
126	                    continue
127	                processor_str = r.custom_logit_processor
128	                if processor_str not in processor_dict:
129	                    processor_dict[processor_str] = []
130	                processor_dict[processor_str].append(i)
131	
132	            merged_custom_logit_processor = {
133	                hash(processor_str): (
134	                    # The deserialized custom logit processor object
135	                    CustomLogitProcessor.from_str(processor_str),
136	                    # The mask tensor for the requests that use this custom logit processor
137	                    torch.zeros(len(reqs), dtype=torch.bool)
138	                    .scatter_(0, torch.tensor(true_indices), True)
139	                    .to(device, non_blocking=True),
140	                )
141	                for processor_str, true_indices in processor_dict.items()
142	            }
143	            custom_params = [r.sampling_params.custom_params for r in reqs]
144	        else:
145	            merged_custom_logit_processor = None
146	            custom_params = None
147	
148	        # Each penalizers will do nothing if they evaluate themselves as not required by looking at
149	        # the sampling_params of the requests (See {_is_required()} of each penalizers). So this
150	        # should not add hefty computation overhead other than simple checks.
151	        #
152	        # While we can choose not to even create the class instances if they are not required, this
153	        # could add additional complexity to the {ScheduleBatch} class, especially we need to
154	        # handle {filter_batch()} and {merge_batch()} cases as well.

156	            vocab_size=vocab_size,
157	            batch=batch,
158	            penalizers={
159	                penaltylib.BatchedFrequencyPenalizer,
160	                penaltylib.BatchedMinNewTokensPenalizer,
161	                penaltylib.BatchedPresencePenalizer,
162	            },
163	        )

167	            top_ps=top_ps,
168	            top_ks=top_ks,
169	            min_ps=min_ps,
170	            sampling_seed=sampling_seed,
171	            is_all_greedy=all(r.sampling_params.top_k <= 1 for r in reqs),
172	            need_top_p_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.top_p != 1.0 for r in reqs),
173	            need_top_k_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.top_k != TOP_K_ALL for r in reqs),
174	            need_min_p_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.min_p > 0 for r in reqs),
175	            vocab_size=vocab_size,
176	            penalizer_orchestrator=penalizer_orchestrator,
177	            has_custom_logit_processor=has_custom_logit_processor,
178	            custom_params=custom_params,
179	            custom_logit_processor=merged_custom_logit_processor,
180	            device=device,
181	            logit_bias=logit_bias,
182	        )
183	        ret.adjusted_from_schedule_batch(batch, vocab_size)

201	            return

208	            vocab_size=self.vocab_size,

210	            device=self.device,
211	        )

213	            first_grammar.apply_vocab_mask
214	        )  # force to use static method
215	
216	        # Apply the mask
217	        for i, grammar in enumerate(self.grammars):
218	            if grammar and not grammar.finished and not grammar.is_terminated():
219	                grammar.fill_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, i)

296	        device: str,
297	    ):
298	        if lhs is None and rhs is None:
299	            return None
300	        lhs, rhs = lhs or {}, rhs or {}
301	
302	        keys = set(lhs.keys()).union(set(rhs.keys()))
303	        merged_dict = {}
304	
305	        for k in keys:
306	            # Get the logit processor object
307	            processor = lhs[k][0] if k in lhs else rhs[k][0]
308	            # Get and merge the mask tensors from the two dicts
309	            left_mask = (
310	                lhs[k][1]
311	                if k in lhs
312	                else torch.zeros(bs1, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
313	            )
314	            right_mask = (
315	                rhs[k][1]
316	                if k in rhs
317	                else torch.zeros(bs2, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
318	            )
319	            merged_dict[k] = (processor, torch.cat([left_mask, right_mask]))
320	
321	            assert merged_dict[k][1].shape[0] == bs1 + bs2, (
322	                f"The batch size of merged mask ({merged_dict[k][1].shape[0]}) does not match "
323	                f"the sum of the batch sizes of the two masks ({bs1 + bs2})"
324	                f"\n{left_mask=}\n{right_mask=}\n{bs1=}\n{bs2=}"
325	                f"\n{lhs=}\n{rhs=}"
326	            )
327	
328	        return merged_dict
329	
330	    def merge_batch(self, other: "SamplingBatchInfo"):
331	        self.penalizer_orchestrator.merge(other.penalizer_orchestrator)
332	
333	        # Merge the custom logit processors and custom params lists
334	        if self.has_custom_logit_processor or other.has_custom_logit_processor:
335	            # Merge the custom logit processors
336	            self.custom_logit_processor = (
337	                SamplingBatchInfo.merge_custom_logit_processor(
338	                    self.custom_logit_processor,
339	                    other.custom_logit_processor,
340	                    len(self),
341	                    len(other),
342	                    self.device,
343	                )
344	            )
345	            # Merge the custom params lists
346	            self.custom_params = self.custom_params or [None] * len(self)
347	            other.custom_params = other.custom_params or [None] * len(other)
348	            self.custom_params.extend(other.custom_params)
349	
350	            # Set the flag to True if any of the two has custom logit processor
351	            self.has_custom_logit_processor = True
352	
353	        # Merge logit bias - note this has to come before the temperatures tensor update! Otherwise will cause crashes.
354	        # See note below on len(self) and len(other).
355	        self.logit_bias = merge_bias_tensor(
356	            self.logit_bias, other.logit_bias, len(self), len(other), self.device, 0.0
357	        )
358	
359	        # Note: because the __len()__ operator is defined on the temperatures tensor,
360	        # please make sure any merge operation with len(self) or len(other) is done before
361	        # the merge operation of the temperatures tensor below.
362	        for item in [
363	            "temperatures",
364	            "top_ps",
365	            "top_ks",
366	            "min_ps",
367	            "sampling_seed",
368	        ]:
369	            self_val = getattr(self, item, None)
370	            other_val = getattr(other, item, None)
371	            if self_val is not None and other_val is not None:
372	                setattr(self, item, torch.cat([self_val, other_val]))
373	
374	        self.is_all_greedy &= other.is_all_greedy
375	        self.need_top_p_sampling |= other.need_top_p_sampling
376	        self.need_top_k_sampling |= other.need_top_k_sampling
377	        self.need_min_p_sampling |= other.need_min_p_sampling
378	
379	        self.adjusted_merge_batch(other)
380	
381	    def copy_for_forward(self):
382	        # Accumulate the penalty into a pre-allocated buffer to get rid of the dependency of `penalizer_orchestrator` later
383	        self.update_penalties()
384	        return dataclasses.replace(self, penalizer_orchestrator=None)
385	
386	
387	def merge_bias_tensor(
388	    lhs: Optional[torch.Tensor],
389	    rhs: Optional[torch.Tensor],
390	    bs1: int,
391	    bs2: int,
392	    device: str,
393	    default: float,
394	):
395	    """Merge two bias tensors for batch merging.
396	
397	    Args:
398	        lhs: Left-hand side tensor
399	        rhs: Right-hand side tensor
400	        bs1: Batch size of left-hand side tensor
401	        bs2: Batch size of right-hand side tensor
402	        device: Device to place the merged tensor on
403	        default: Default value for missing tensor elements
404	
405	    Returns:
406	        Merged tensor or None if both inputs are None
407	    """
408	    if lhs is None and rhs is None:
409	        return None
410	
411	    if lhs is not None and rhs is not None:
412	        return torch.cat([lhs, rhs])
413	    else:
414	        if lhs is not None:
415	            shape, dtype = lhs.shape[1:], lhs.dtype
416	        else:
417	            shape, dtype = rhs.shape[1:], rhs.dtype
418	
419	        if lhs is None:
420	            lhs = torch.empty((bs1, *shape), device=device, dtype=dtype).fill_(default)
421	        if rhs is None:
422	            rhs = torch.empty((bs2, *shape), device=device, dtype=dtype).fill_(default)
423	        return torch.cat([lhs, rhs])
424

12	def _find_common_prefix(s1: str, s2: str) -> str:
13	    prefix = ""
14	    min_length = min(len(s1), len(s2))
15	    for i in range(0, min_length):
16	        if s1[i] == s2[i]:
17	            prefix += s1[i]
18	        else:
19	            break
20	    return prefix

23	def _partial_json_loads(input_str: str, flags: Allow) -> Tuple[Any, int]:
24	    """
25	    Parse incomplete or partial JSON strings commonly encountered during streaming.
26	
27	    Args:
28	        input_str (str): The potentially incomplete JSON string to parse.
29	        flags (Allow): Bitwise flags controlling what types of partial data are allowed.
30	            Common flags include:
31	            - Allow.STR: Allow partial strings (e.g., '"hello wo' -> 'hello wo')
32	            - Allow.OBJ: Allow partial objects (e.g., '{"key":' -> {'key': None})
33	            - Allow.ARR: Allow partial arrays (e.g., '[1, 2,' -> [1, 2])
34	            - Allow.ALL: Allow all types of partial data
35	
36	    Returns:
37	        Tuple[Any, int]: A tuple containing:
38	            - parsed_object: The Python object parsed from the JSON
39	            - consumed_length: Number of characters consumed from input_str
40	    """
41	    try:
42	        return (partial_json_parser.loads(input_str, flags), len(input_str))
43	    except (JSONDecodeError, IndexError) as e:
44	        msg = getattr(e, "msg", str(e))
45	        if "Extra data" in msg or "pop from empty list" in msg:
46	            start = WHITESPACE.match(input_str, 0).end()
47	            obj, end = JSONDecoder().raw_decode(input_str, start)
48	            return obj, end
49	        raise
50	
51	
52	def _is_complete_json(input_str: str) -> bool:
53	    try:
54	        orjson.loads(input_str)
55	        return True
56	    except JSONDecodeError:
57	        return False

60	def _get_tool_schema_defs(tools: List[Tool]) -> dict:
61	    """
62	    Get consolidated $defs from all tools, validating for conflicts.
63	
64	    Args:
65	        tools: List of tools to process
66	
67	    Returns:
68	        Dictionary of consolidated $defs from all tools
69	
70	    Raises:
71	        ValueError: If conflicting $defs are found
72	    """
73	    all_defs = {}
74	    for tool in tools:
75	        if tool.function.parameters is None:
76	            continue
77	        defs = tool.function.parameters.get("$defs", {})
78	        for def_name, def_schema in defs.items():
79	            if def_name in all_defs and all_defs[def_name] != def_schema:
80	                raise ValueError(
81	                    f"Tool definition '{def_name}' has "
82	                    "multiple schemas, which is not "
83	                    "supported."
84	                )
85	            else:
86	                all_defs[def_name] = def_schema
87	    return all_defs
88	
89	
90	def _get_tool_schema(tool: Tool) -> dict:
91	    return {
92	        "properties": {
93	            "name": {"type": "string", "enum": [tool.function.name]},
94	            "parameters": (
95	                tool.function.parameters
96	                if tool.function.parameters
97	                else {"type": "object", "properties": {}}
98	            ),
99	        },
100	        "required": ["name", "parameters"],
101	    }
102	
103	
104	def infer_type_from_json_schema(schema: Dict[str, Any]) -> Optional[str]:
105	    """
106	    Infer the primary type of a parameter from JSON Schema.
107	
108	    Supports complex JSON Schema structures including:
109	    - Direct type field (including type arrays)
110	    - anyOf/oneOf: parameter can be any of multiple types
111	    - enum: parameter must be one of enum values
112	    - allOf: parameter must satisfy all type definitions
113	    - properties: inferred as object type
114	    - items: inferred as array type
115	
116	    Args:
117	        schema: JSON Schema definition
118	
119	    Returns:
120	        Inferred type ('string', 'number', 'object', 'array', etc.) or None
121	    """
122	    if not isinstance(schema, dict):
123	        return None
124	
125	    # Priority 1: Direct type field (including type arrays)
126	    if "type" in schema:
127	        type_value = schema["type"]
128	        if isinstance(type_value, str):
129	            return type_value
130	        elif isinstance(type_value, list) and type_value:
131	            # Handle type arrays: return first non-null type
132	            non_null_types = [t for t in type_value if t != "null"]
133	            if non_null_types:
134	                return non_null_types[0]
135	            return "string"  # If only null, default to string
136	
137	    # Priority 2: Handle anyOf/oneOf
138	    if "anyOf" in schema or "oneOf" in schema:
139	        schemas = schema.get("anyOf") or schema.get("oneOf")
140	        types = []
141	
142	        if isinstance(schemas, list):
143	            for sub_schema in schemas:
144	                inferred_type = infer_type_from_json_schema(sub_schema)
145	                if inferred_type:
146	                    types.append(inferred_type)
147	
148	            if types:
149	                # If all types are the same, return unified type
150	                if len(set(types)) == 1:
151	                    return types[0]
152	                # When types differ, prioritize string (safest)
153	                if "string" in types:
154	                    return "string"
155	                # Otherwise return first type
156	                return types[0]
157	
158	    # Priority 3: Handle enum (infer type from enum values)
159	    if "enum" in schema and isinstance(schema["enum"], list):
160	        if not schema["enum"]:
161	            return "string"
162	
163	        # Infer type from enum values
164	        enum_types = set()
165	        for value in schema["enum"]:
166	            if value is None:
167	                enum_types.add("null")
168	            elif isinstance(value, bool):
169	                enum_types.add("boolean")
170	            elif isinstance(value, int):
171	                enum_types.add("integer")
172	            elif isinstance(value, float):
173	                enum_types.add("number")
174	            elif isinstance(value, str):
175	                enum_types.add("string")
176	            elif isinstance(value, list):
177	                enum_types.add("array")
178	            elif isinstance(value, dict):
179	                enum_types.add("object")
180	
181	        # If type is uniform, return that type
182	        if len(enum_types) == 1:
183	            return enum_types.pop()
184	        # Mixed types, prioritize string
185	        return "string"
186	
187	    # Priority 4: Handle allOf (must satisfy all types)
188	    if "allOf" in schema and isinstance(schema["allOf"], list):
189	        schemas = schema["allOf"]
190	        for sub_schema in schemas:
191	            inferred_type = infer_type_from_json_schema(sub_schema)
192	            if inferred_type and inferred_type != "string":
193	                return inferred_type
194	        return "string"
195	
196	    # Priority 5: Infer object type
197	    if "properties" in schema:
198	        return "object"
199	
200	    # Priority 6: Infer array type
201	    if "items" in schema:
202	        return "array"
203	
204	    return None
205	
206	
207	def get_json_schema_constraint(
208	    tools: List[Tool], tool_choice: Union[ToolChoice, Literal["required"]]
209	) -> Optional[dict]:
210	    """
211	    Get the JSON schema constraint for the specified tool choice.
212	
213	    Args:
214	        tool_choice: The tool choice specification
215	
216	    Returns:
217	        JSON schema dict, or None if no valid tools found
218	    """
219	
220	    if isinstance(tool_choice, ToolChoice):
221	        # For specific function choice, return the user's parameters schema directly
222	        fn_name = tool_choice.function.name
223	        for tool in tools:
224	            if tool.function.name == fn_name:
225	                return {
226	                    "type": "array",
227	                    "minItems": 1,
228	                    "maxItems": 1,
229	                    "items": _get_tool_schema(tool),
230	                }
231	        return None
232	    elif tool_choice == "required":
233	        json_schema = {
234	            "type": "array",
235	            "minItems": 1,
236	            "items": {
237	                "type": "object",
238	                "anyOf": [_get_tool_schema(tool) for tool in tools],
239	            },
240	        }
241	        json_schema_defs = _get_tool_schema_defs(tools)
242	        if json_schema_defs:
243	            json_schema["$defs"] = json_schema_defs
244	        return json_schema
245	
246	    return None

14	"""The baseclass of a backend for reasoner grammar-guided constrained decoding."""
15	
16	from typing import List, Optional, Tuple
17	
18	import torch
19	
20	from .base_grammar_backend import (
21	    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
22	    BaseGrammarBackend,
23	    BaseGrammarObject,
24	)
25	
26	
27	class ReasonerGrammarObject(BaseGrammarObject):
28	    def __init__(self, grammar: BaseGrammarObject, think_end_id: int):
29	        super().__init__()
30	        self.grammar = grammar
31	        self.think_end_id = think_end_id
32	        # -1    means thinking has not ended yet
33	        # 0     means just ended thinking in the last token
34	        # +     means number of tokens after thinking ended
35	        self.tokens_after_think_end = -1
36	
37	    def maybe_init_reasoning(self, reasoning: bool):
38	        self.tokens_after_think_end = -1 if reasoning else 0
39	
40	    def transfer_state(self, token: int) -> int:
41	        if self.tokens_after_think_end == -1 and token == self.think_end_id:
42	            self.tokens_after_think_end = 0
43	        elif self.tokens_after_think_end >= 0:
44	            self.tokens_after_think_end += 1
45	
46	    def rollback_state(self):
47	        if self.tokens_after_think_end == 0:
48	            self.tokens_after_think_end = -1
49	        elif self.tokens_after_think_end > 0:
50	            self.tokens_after_think_end -= 1
51	
52	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
53	        if self.tokens_after_think_end >= 0:
54	            self.grammar.accept_token(token)
55	        self.transfer_state(token)
56	
57	    def is_terminated(self):
58	        return self.grammar.is_terminated()
59	
60	    def rollback(self, k):
61	        steps_after_think = min(k, self.tokens_after_think_end)
62	        if steps_after_think > 0:
63	            self.grammar.rollback(steps_after_think)
64	
65	        for _ in range(k):
66	            self.rollback_state()
67	
68	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
69	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
70	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
71	        return self.grammar.allocate_vocab_mask(vocab_size, batch_size, device)
72	
73	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
74	        if self.tokens_after_think_end >= 0:
75	            self.grammar.fill_vocab_mask(vocab_mask, idx)
76	
77	    def move_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
78	        return self.grammar.move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask, device)
79	
80	    @property
81	    def apply_vocab_mask(self):
82	        return self.grammar.apply_vocab_mask
83	
84	    def copy(self) -> BaseGrammarObject:
85	        return ReasonerGrammarObject(self.grammar.copy(), self.think_end_id)
86	
87	    @property
88	    def finished(self):
89	        return self.grammar.finished
90	
91	    @finished.setter
92	    def finished(self, finished):
93	        self.grammar.finished = finished
94	
95	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer):
96	        return self.grammar.try_jump_forward(tokenizer)
97	
98	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper):
99	        return self.grammar.jump_forward_str_state(helper)
100	
101	    def jump_and_retokenize(
102	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
103	    ):
104	        return self.grammar.jump_and_retokenize(
105	            old_output_ids, new_output_ids, next_state
106	        )
107	
108	
109	class ReasonerGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
110	    def __init__(self, grammar_backend: BaseGrammarBackend, think_end_id):
111	        super().__init__()
112	        self.grammar_backend = grammar_backend
113	        self.think_end_id = think_end_id
114	
115	    def _init_value_dispatch(
116	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], reasoning: bool
117	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
118	        ret = self.grammar_backend._init_value_dispatch(key, reasoning)
119	        # avoid wrapping invalid grammar, so that the scheduler can detect it
120	        if ret is None or ret is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:
121	            return ret
122	        obj = ReasonerGrammarObject(ret, self.think_end_id)
123	        obj.maybe_init_reasoning(reasoning)
124	        return obj

28	)

40	class GuidanceGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
41	
42	    def __init__(self, llguidance_tokenizer: LLTokenizer, serialized_grammar: str):
43	        super().__init__()
44	        self.llguidance_tokenizer = llguidance_tokenizer
45	        self.serialized_grammar = serialized_grammar
46	
47	        self.ll_matcher = LLMatcher(
48	            self.llguidance_tokenizer,
49	            self.serialized_grammar,
50	            log_level=int(os.environ.get("LLGUIDANCE_LOG_LEVEL", "1")),
51	        )
52	        self.bitmask = None
53	
54	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
55	        if not self.ll_matcher.consume_token(token):
56	            logger.warning(f"matcher error: {self.ll_matcher.get_error()}")
57	            self.finished = True
58	
59	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
60	        if self.ll_matcher.is_stopped():
61	            self.finished = True
62	
63	        fill_next_token_bitmask(self.ll_matcher, vocab_mask, idx)
64	
65	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
66	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
67	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
68	        if self.bitmask is None or self.bitmask.shape[0] < batch_size:
69	            # only create bitmask when batch gets larger
70	            self.bitmask = allocate_token_bitmask(
71	                batch_size, self.llguidance_tokenizer.vocab_size
72	            )
73	            bitmask = self.bitmask
74	        else:
75	            bitmask = self.bitmask[:batch_size]
76	
77	        return bitmask
78	
79	    @staticmethod
80	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
81	        return vocab_mask.to(device, non_blocking=True)
82	
83	    @staticmethod
84	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
